5671 // value (d) for generating a single cmpxchg/atomicrmw.
5672 if (isAtomicComparePatternOp(op))
5673 continue;
5674
5675 // Avoid translating ops that depend on the unmapped block argument.
5676 bool allOperandsMapped = llvm::all_of(op.getOperands(), [&](mlir::Value v) {
5677 return moduleTranslation.lookupValue(v) != nullptr;
5678 });
5679 if (!allOperandsMapped)
5680 continue;
5681
5682 if (failed(moduleTranslation.convertOperation(op, builder)))
5683 return atomicCompareOp.emitError(
5684 "failed to translate operation inside atomic compare region");
5685 }
5686
5687 // Look up a value that may have been pre-translated or defined outside the
5688 // region.
5689 auto materializeValue = [&](mlir::Value val) -> llvm::Value * {
5690 // Check if the value is already mapped (pre-translated or defined outside).
5691 if (llvm::Value *existing = moduleTranslation.lookupValue(val))
5692 return existing;
5693 // Fallback for a single LoadOp whose address is mapped but whose result
5694 // was not pre-translated.
5695 if (auto loadOp = val.getDefiningOp<LLVM::LoadOp>()) {
5696 if (loadOp->getParentRegion() == &region) {
5697 llvm::Value *loadAddr = moduleTranslation.lookupValue(loadOp.getAddr());
5698 if (!loadAddr)
5699 return nullptr;
5700 llvm::Type *loadType =
5701 moduleTranslation.convertType(loadOp.getResult().getType());
5702 return builder.CreateLoad(loadType, loadAddr);
5703 }
5704 }
5705 return nullptr;
5706 };
5707
5708 // Walk the region to extract comparison predicate, eVal, and dVal.
5709 // if (x == eVal) x = dVal
5710 llvm::omp::OMPAtomicCompareOp compareOp = llvm::omp::OMPAtomicCompareOp::EQ;
5711 llvm::Value *eVal = nullptr;
5712 llvm::Value *dVal = nullptr;
5713 bool isXBinopExpr = false;
5714
5715 auto traceToAggregate = [](mlir::Value v) -> mlir::Value {
5716 if (auto extractOp = v.getDefiningOp<LLVM::ExtractValueOp>())
5717 return extractOp.getContainer();
5718 return nullptr;
5719 };
5720
5721 // Check for a decomposed complex comparison pattern:
5722 // %re_x = llvm.extractvalue %xval[0]
5723 // %re_e = llvm.extractvalue %eStruct[0]
5724 // %cmp_re = llvm.fcmp "oeq" %re_x, %re_e
5725 // %im_x = llvm.extractvalue %xval[1]
5726 // %im_e = llvm.extractvalue %eStruct[1]
5727 // %cmp_im = llvm.fcmp "oeq" %im_x, %im_e
5728 // %cmp = llvm.and %cmp_re, %cmp_im (for EQ)
5729 // Detect this by looking for AndOp/OrOp whose operands are both FCmpOps
5730 // operating on ExtractValueOps from the block argument.
5731 bool isComplexPattern = false;
5732 for (Operation &op : block.getOperations()) {
5733 if (!isa<LLVM::AndOp, LLVM::OrOp>(op))
5734 continue;
5735
5736 // Using : %cmp = llvm.and %cmp_re, %cmp_im
5737 auto lhsFcmp = op.getOperand(0).getDefiningOp<LLVM::FCmpOp>();
5738 auto rhsFcmp = op.getOperand(1).getDefiningOp<LLVM::FCmpOp>();
5739 if (!lhsFcmp || !rhsFcmp)
5740 continue;
5741
5742 // Using : %cmp_re = llvm.fcmp "oeq" %re_x, %re_e
5743 // Check presence of x (block argument) and get e.
5744 mlir::Value lhsAgg0 = traceToAggregate(lhsFcmp.getOperand(0));
5745 mlir::Value lhsAgg1 = traceToAggregate(lhsFcmp.getOperand(1));
5746 bool lhsXIsOp0 = (lhsAgg0 == block.getArgument(0));
5747 bool lhsXIsOp1 = (lhsAgg1 == block.getArgument(0));
5748 if (!lhsXIsOp0 && !lhsXIsOp1)
5749 continue;
5750 mlir::Value eAggregate = lhsXIsOp0 ? lhsAgg1 : lhsAgg0;
5751 if (!eAggregate)
5752 continue;
5753
5754 if (isa<LLVM::AndOp>(op))
5755 compareOp = llvm::omp::OMPAtomicCompareOp::EQ;
5756 else
5757 // OrOp corresponds to NE, which is not a valid atomic compare op.
5758 return atomicCompareOp.emitError(
5759 "unsupported comparison predicate (NE) for complex atomic compare");
5760
5761 isXBinopExpr = lhsXIsOp0;
5762 eVal = materializeValue(eAggregate);
5763 isComplexPattern = true;
5764 break;
5765 }
5766
5767 if (isComplexPattern) {
5768 // dVal from SelectOp or YieldOp.
5769 for (Operation &op : block.getOperations()) {
5770 if (auto selectOp = dyn_cast<LLVM::SelectOp>(op)) {
5771 dVal = materializeValue(selectOp.getTrueValue());
5772 break;
5773 }
5774 }
5775 if (!dVal) {
5776 auto yieldOp = cast<omp::YieldOp>(block.getTerminator());
5777 if (yieldOp.getResults().empty())
5778 return atomicCompareOp.emitError(
5779 "failed to extract desired value (d) from atomic compare region");
5780 dVal = materializeValue(yieldOp.getResults()[0]);
5781 }
5782
5783 const llvm::DataLayout &DL =
5784 builder.GetInsertBlock()->getModule()->getDataLayout();
5785 unsigned totalBits =
5786 DL.getTypeStoreSizeInBits(llvmXElementType).getFixedValue();
5787
5788 llvm::IntegerType *intTy =
5789 llvm::IntegerType::get(builder.getContext(), totalBits);
5790
5791 llvm::Align complexAlign = DL.getABITypeAlign(llvmXElementType);
5792 llvm::Align intAlign = DL.getABITypeAlign(intTy);
5793 llvm::Align maxAlign = std::max(complexAlign, intAlign);
5794
5795 llvm::AllocaInst *eAlloca =
5796 builder.CreateAlloca(llvmXElementType, nullptr, "cmplx.e");
5797 eAlloca->setAlignment(maxAlign);
5798 llvm::AllocaInst *dAlloca =
5799 builder.CreateAlloca(llvmXElementType, nullptr, "cmplx.d");
5800 dAlloca->setAlignment(maxAlign);
5801
5802 builder.CreateAlignedStore(eVal, eAlloca, maxAlign);
5803 llvm::Value *eInt =
5804 builder.CreateAlignedLoad(intTy, eAlloca, maxAlign, "cmplx.e.int");
5805 builder.CreateAlignedStore(dVal, dAlloca, maxAlign);
5806 llvm::Value *dInt =
5807 builder.CreateAlignedLoad(intTy, dAlloca, maxAlign, "cmplx.d.int");
5808
5809 llvm::AtomicOrdering failOrdering =
5810 llvm::AtomicCmpXchgInst::getStrongestFailureOrdering(atomicOrdering);
5811 auto *cmpXchg = builder.CreateAtomicCmpXchg(llvmX, eInt, dInt, maxAlign,
5812 atomicOrdering, failOrdering);
5813 cmpXchg->setWeak(atomicCompareOp.getWeak());
5814
5815 // Emit flush after atomic compare if needed (for release, acq_rel,
5816 // seq_cst orderings).
5817 if (atomicOrdering == llvm::AtomicOrdering::Release ||
5818 atomicOrdering == llvm::AtomicOrdering::AcquireRelease ||
5819 atomicOrdering == llvm::AtomicOrdering::SequentiallyConsistent) {
5820 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::LocationDescription ompLoc(builder);
5821 ompBuilder->createFlush(ompLoc);
5822 }
5823 return success();
5824 } else {
5825
5826 for (Operation &op : block.getOperations()) {
5827 if (auto icmpOp = dyn_cast<LLVM::ICmpOp>(op)) {
5828 auto maybeOp =
5829 convertICmpPredicateToAtomicCompareOp(icmpOp.getPredicate());
5830 if (!maybeOp)
5831 return atomicCompareOp.emitError(
5832 "unsupported comparison predicate in atomic compare");
5833 compareOp = *maybeOp;
5834
5835 LLVM::ICmpPredicate pred = icmpOp.getPredicate();
5836 isSigned = (pred == LLVM::ICmpPredicate::slt ||
5837 pred == LLVM::ICmpPredicate::sgt ||
5838 pred == LLVM::ICmpPredicate::sle ||
5839 pred == LLVM::ICmpPredicate::sge);
5840
5841 // Identify which operand is the block argument (x) and which is e.
5842 isXBinopExpr = (icmpOp.getOperand(0) == block.getArgument(0));
5843 mlir::Value eOperand =
5844 isXBinopExpr ? icmpOp.getOperand(1) : icmpOp.getOperand(0);
5845 eVal = materializeValue(eOperand);
5846 } else if (auto fcmpOp = dyn_cast<LLVM::FCmpOp>(op)) {
5847 auto maybeOp =
5848 convertFCmpPredicateToAtomicCompareOp(fcmpOp.getPredicate());
5849 if (!maybeOp)
5850 return atomicCompareOp.emitError(
5851 "unsupported comparison predicate in atomic compare");
5852 compareOp = *maybeOp;
5853
5854 isXBinopExpr = (fcmpOp.getOperand(0) == block.getArgument(0));
5855 mlir::Value eOperand =
5856 isXBinopExpr ? fcmpOp.getOperand(1) : fcmpOp.getOperand(0);
5857 eVal = materializeValue(eOperand);
5858 } else if (auto selectOp = dyn_cast<LLVM::SelectOp>(op)) {
5859 if (!dVal)
5860 dVal = materializeValue(selectOp.getTrueValue());
5861 }
5862 }
5863 }
5864
5865 // For non-complex patterns, also extract dVal from SelectOp.
5866 if (!dVal) {
5867 for (Operation &op : block.getOperations()) {
5868 if (auto selectOp = dyn_cast<LLVM::SelectOp>(op)) {
5869 dVal = materializeValue(selectOp.getTrueValue());
5870 break;
5871 }
5872 }
5873 }
5874
5875 if (!eVal)
5876 return atomicCompareOp.emitError(
5877 "failed to extract expected value (e) from atomic compare region");
5878 if (!dVal) {
5879 // Fall back to the yield operand.
5880 auto yieldOp = cast<omp::YieldOp>(block.getTerminator());
5881 if (yieldOp.getResults().empty())
5882 return atomicCompareOp.emitError(
5883 "failed to extract desired value (d) from atomic compare region");
5884 dVal = materializeValue(yieldOp.getResults()[0]);
5885 }
5886
5887 llvmAtomicX.IsSigned = isSigned;
5888
5889 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::AtomicOpValue vOpVal = {nullptr, nullptr, false,
5890 false};
5891 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::AtomicOpValue rOpVal = {nullptr, nullptr, false,
5892 false};
5893 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::LocationDescription ompLoc(builder);
5894
5895 bool isWeak = atomicCompareOp.getWeak();
5896
5897 bool savedHandleFPNegZero = ompBuilder->setHandleFPNegZero(true);
5898 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::InsertPointOrErrorTy afterIP =
5899 ompBuilder->createAtomicCompare(ompLoc, llvmAtomicX, vOpVal, rOpVal, eVal,
5900 dVal, atomicOrdering, compareOp,
5901 isXBinopExpr, false, false, isWeak);
5902 ompBuilder->setHandleFPNegZero(savedHandleFPNegZero);
5903
5904 if (failed(handleError(afterIP, *atomicCompareOp)))
5905 return failure();
5906
5907 builder.restoreIP(*afterIP);
5908 return success();
5909}
5910
5911static llvm::omp::Directive convertCancellationConstructType(
5912 omp::ClauseCancellationConstructType directive) {
5913 switch (directive) {
5914 case omp::ClauseCancellationConstructType::Loop:
5915 return llvm::omp::Directive::OMPD_for;
5916 case omp::ClauseCancellationConstructType::Parallel:
5917 return llvm::omp::Directive::OMPD_parallel;
5918 case omp::ClauseCancellationConstructType::Sections:
5919 return llvm::omp::Directive::OMPD_sections;
5920 case omp::ClauseCancellationConstructType::Taskgroup:
5921 return llvm::omp::Directive::OMPD_taskgroup;
5922 }
5923 llvm_unreachable("Unhandled cancellation construct type");
5924}
5925
5926static LogicalResult
5927convertOmpCancel(omp::CancelOp op, llvm::IRBuilderBase &builder,
5928 LLVM::ModuleTranslation &moduleTranslation) {
5929 if (failed(checkImplementationStatus(*op.getOperation())))
5930 return failure();
5931
5932 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::LocationDescription ompLoc(builder);
5933 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder *ompBuilder = moduleTranslation.getOpenMPBuilder();
5934
5935 llvm::Value *ifCond = nullptr;
5936 if (Value ifVar = op.getIfExpr())
5937 ifCond = moduleTranslation.lookupValue(ifVar);
5938
5939 llvm::omp::Directive cancelledDirective =
5940 convertCancellationConstructType(op.getCancelDirective());
5941
5942 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::InsertPointOrErrorTy afterIP =
5943 ompBuilder->createCancel(ompLoc, ifCond, cancelledDirective);
5944
5945 if (failed(handleError(afterIP, *op.getOperation())))
5946 return failure();
5947
5948 builder.restoreIP(afterIP.get());
5949
5950 return success();
5951}
5952
5953static LogicalResult
5954convertOmpCancellationPoint(omp::CancellationPointOp op,
5955 llvm::IRBuilderBase &builder,
5956 LLVM::ModuleTranslation &moduleTranslation) {
5957 if (failed(checkImplementationStatus(*op.getOperation())))
5958 return failure();
5959
5960 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::LocationDescription ompLoc(builder);
5961 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder *ompBuilder = moduleTranslation.getOpenMPBuilder();
5962
5963 llvm::omp::Directive cancelledDirective =
5964 convertCancellationConstructType(op.getCancelDirective());
5965
5966 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::InsertPointOrErrorTy afterIP =
5967 ompBuilder->createCancellationPoint(ompLoc, cancelledDirective);
5968
5969 if (failed(handleError(afterIP, *op.getOperation())))
5970 return failure();
5971
5972 builder.restoreIP(afterIP.get());
5973
5974 return success();
5975}
5976
5977/// Converts an OpenMP Threadprivate operation into LLVM IR using
5978/// OpenMPIRBuilder.
5979static LogicalResult
5980convertOmpThreadprivate(Operation &opInst, llvm::IRBuilderBase &builder,
5981 LLVM::ModuleTranslation &moduleTranslation) {
5982 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::LocationDescription ompLoc(builder);
5983 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder *ompBuilder = moduleTranslation.getOpenMPBuilder();
5984 auto threadprivateOp = cast<omp::ThreadprivateOp>(opInst);
5985
5986 if (failed(checkImplementationStatus(opInst)))
5987 return failure();
5988
5989 Value symAddr = threadprivateOp.getSymAddr();
5990 auto *symOp = symAddr.getDefiningOp();
5991
5992 if (auto asCast = dyn_cast<LLVM::AddrSpaceCastOp>(symOp))
5993 symOp = asCast.getOperand().getDefiningOp();
5994
5995 if (!isa<LLVM::AddressOfOp>(symOp))
5996 return opInst.emitError("Addressing symbol not found");
5997 LLVM::AddressOfOp addressOfOp = dyn_cast<LLVM::AddressOfOp>(symOp);
5998
5999 LLVM::GlobalOp global =
6000 addressOfOp.getGlobal(moduleTranslation.symbolTable());
6001 llvm::GlobalValue *globalValue = moduleTranslation.lookupGlobal(global);
6002 llvm::Type *type = globalValue->getValueType();
6003 llvm::TypeSize typeSize =
6004 builder.GetInsertBlock()->getModule()->getDataLayout().getTypeStoreSize(
6005 type);
6006 llvm::ConstantInt *size = builder.getInt64(typeSize.getFixedValue());
6007 llvm::Value *callInst = ompBuilder->createCachedThreadPrivate(
6008 ompLoc, globalValue, size, global.getSymName() + ".cache");
6009 moduleTranslation.mapValue(opInst.getResult(0), callInst);
6010
6011 return success();
6012}
6013
6014static llvm::OffloadEntriesInfoManager::OMPTargetDeviceClauseKind
6015convertToDeviceClauseKind(mlir::omp::DeclareTargetDeviceType deviceClause) {
6016 switch (deviceClause) {
6017 case mlir::omp::DeclareTargetDeviceType::host:
6018 return llvm::OffloadEntriesInfoManager::OMPTargetDeviceClauseHost;
6019 break;
6020 case mlir::omp::DeclareTargetDeviceType::nohost:
6021 return llvm::OffloadEntriesInfoManager::OMPTargetDeviceClauseNoHost;
6022 break;
6023 case mlir::omp::DeclareTargetDeviceType::any:
6024 return llvm::OffloadEntriesInfoManager::OMPTargetDeviceClauseAny;
6025 break;
6026 }
6027 llvm_unreachable("unhandled device clause");
6028}
6029
6030static llvm::OffloadEntriesInfoManager::OMPTargetGlobalVarEntryKind
6031convertToCaptureClauseKind(
6032 mlir::omp::DeclareTargetCaptureClause captureClause) {
6033 switch (captureClause) {
6034 case mlir::omp::DeclareTargetCaptureClause::to:
6035 return llvm::OffloadEntriesInfoManager::OMPTargetGlobalVarEntryTo;
6036 case mlir::omp::DeclareTargetCaptureClause::link:
6037 return llvm::OffloadEntriesInfoManager::OMPTargetGlobalVarEntryLink;
6038 case mlir::omp::DeclareTargetCaptureClause::enter:
6039 return llvm::OffloadEntriesInfoManager::OMPTargetGlobalVarEntryEnter;
6040 case mlir::omp::DeclareTargetCaptureClause::none:
6041 return llvm::OffloadEntriesInfoManager::OMPTargetGlobalVarEntryNone;
6042 }
6043 llvm_unreachable("unhandled capture clause");
6044}
6045
6046static Operation *getGlobalOpFromValue(Value value) {
6047 Operation *op = value.getDefiningOp();
6048 if (auto addrCast = dyn_cast_if_present<LLVM::AddrSpaceCastOp>(op))
6049 op = addrCast->getOperand(0).getDefiningOp();
6050 if (auto addressOfOp = dyn_cast_if_present<LLVM::AddressOfOp>(op)) {
6051 auto modOp = addressOfOp->getParentOfType<mlir::ModuleOp>();
6052 return modOp.lookupSymbol(addressOfOp.getGlobalName());
6053 }
6054 return nullptr;
6055}
6056
6057static Value getBaseValueForTypeLookup(Value value) {
6058 while (Operation *op = value.getDefiningOp()) {
6059 if (auto addrCast = dyn_cast_if_present<LLVM::AddrSpaceCastOp>(op))
6060 value = addrCast.getOperand();
6061 // Traces through hlfir.declare, fir.declare to reach the base address and
6062 // use for type lookup.
6063 else if (op->getName().getIdentifier() &&
6064 (op->getName().getIdentifier().str() == "hlfir.declare" ||
6065 op->getName().getIdentifier().str() == "fir.declare")) {
6066 if (op->getNumOperands() > 0)
6067 value = op->getOperand(0);
6068 else
6069 break;
6070 } else {
6071 break;
6072 }
6073 }
6074 return value;
6075}
6076
6077static llvm::SmallString<64>
6078getDeclareTargetRefPtrSuffix(LLVM::GlobalOp globalOp,
6079 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder &ompBuilder,
6080 llvm::vfs::FileSystem &vfs) {
6081 llvm::SmallString<64> suffix;
6082 llvm::raw_svector_ostream os(suffix);
6083 if (globalOp.getVisibility() == mlir::SymbolTable::Visibility::Private) {
6084 auto loc = globalOp->getLoc()->findInstanceOf<FileLineColLoc>();
6085 auto fileInfoCallBack = [&loc]() {
6086 return std::pair<std::string, uint64_t>(
6087 llvm::StringRef(loc.getFilename()), loc.getLine());
6088 };
6089
6090 os << llvm::format(
6091 "_%x",
6092 ompBuilder.getTargetEntryUniqueInfo(fileInfoCallBack, vfs).FileID);
6093 }
6094 os << "_decl_tgt_ref_ptr";
6095
6096 return suffix;
6097}
6098
6099static bool isDeclareTargetLink(Value value) {
6100 if (auto declareTargetGlobal =
6101 dyn_cast_if_present<omp::DeclareTargetInterface>(
6102 getGlobalOpFromValue(value)))
6103 if (declareTargetGlobal.getDeclareTargetCaptureClause() ==
6104 omp::DeclareTargetCaptureClause::link)
6105 return true;
6106 return false;
6107}
6108
6109static bool isDeclareTargetTo(Value value) {
6110 if (auto declareTargetGlobal =
6111 dyn_cast_if_present<omp::DeclareTargetInterface>(
6112 getGlobalOpFromValue(value)))
6113 if (declareTargetGlobal.getDeclareTargetCaptureClause() ==
6114 omp::DeclareTargetCaptureClause::to ||
6115 declareTargetGlobal.getDeclareTargetCaptureClause() ==
6116 omp::DeclareTargetCaptureClause::enter)
6117 return true;
6118 return false;
6119}
6120
6121// Returns the reference pointer generated by the lowering of the declare
6122// target operation in cases where the link clause is used or the to clause is
6123// used in USM mode.
6124static llvm::Value *
6125getRefPtrIfDeclareTarget(Value value,
6126 LLVM::ModuleTranslation &moduleTranslation) {
6127 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder *ompBuilder = moduleTranslation.getOpenMPBuilder();
6128 if (auto gOp =
6129 dyn_cast_or_null<LLVM::GlobalOp>(getGlobalOpFromValue(value))) {
6130 // In this case, we must utilise the reference pointer generated by
6131 // the declare target operation, similar to Clang
6132 if (isDeclareTargetLink(value) ||
6133 (isDeclareTargetTo(value) &&
6134 ompBuilder->Config.hasRequiresUnifiedSharedMemory())) {
6135 llvm::SmallString<64> suffix = getDeclareTargetRefPtrSuffix(
6136 gOp, *ompBuilder, moduleTranslation.getFileSystem());
6137
6138 if (gOp.getSymName().contains(suffix))
6139 return moduleTranslation.getLLVMModule()->getNamedValue(
6140 gOp.getSymName());
6141
6142 return moduleTranslation.getLLVMModule()->getNamedValue(
6143 (gOp.getSymName().str() + suffix.str()).str());
6144 }
6145 }
6146 return nullptr;
6147}
6148
6149namespace {
6150// Append customMappers information to existing MapInfosTy
6151struct MapInfosTy : llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::MapInfosTy {
6152 SmallVector<Operation *, 4> Mappers;
6153
6154 /// Append arrays in \a CurInfo.
6155 void append(MapInfosTy &curInfo) {
6156 Mappers.append(curInfo.Mappers.begin(), curInfo.Mappers.end());
6157 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::MapInfosTy::append(curInfo);
6158 }
6159};
6160// A small helper structure to contain data gathered
6161// for map lowering and coalese it into one area and
6162// avoiding extra computations such as searches in the
6163// llvm module for lowered mapped variables or checking
6164// if something is declare target (and retrieving the
6165// value) more than neccessary.
6166struct MapInfoData : MapInfosTy {
6167 llvm::SmallVector<bool, 4> IsDeclareTarget;
6168 llvm::SmallVector<bool, 4> IsAMember;
6169 // Identify if mapping was added by mapClause or use_device clauses.
6170 llvm::SmallVector<bool, 4> IsAMapping;
6171 llvm::SmallVector<mlir::Operation *, 4> MapClause;
6172 llvm::SmallVector<llvm::Value *, 4> OriginalValue;
6173 // Stripped off array/pointer to get the underlying
6174 // element type
6175 llvm::SmallVector<llvm::Type *, 4> BaseType;
6176
6177 /// Append arrays in \a CurInfo.
6178 void append(MapInfoData &CurInfo) {
6179 IsDeclareTarget.append(CurInfo.IsDeclareTarget.begin(),
6180 CurInfo.IsDeclareTarget.end());
6181 MapClause.append(CurInfo.MapClause.begin(), CurInfo.MapClause.end());
6182 OriginalValue.append(CurInfo.OriginalValue.begin(),
6183 CurInfo.OriginalValue.end());
6184 BaseType.append(CurInfo.BaseType.begin(), CurInfo.BaseType.end());
6185 MapInfosTy::append(CurInfo);
6186 }
6187};
6188
6189enum class TargetDirectiveEnumTy : uint32_t {
6190 None = 0,
6191 Target = 1,
6192 TargetData = 2,
6193 TargetEnterData = 3,
6194 TargetExitData = 4,
6195 TargetUpdate = 5
6196};
6197
6198static TargetDirectiveEnumTy getTargetDirectiveEnumTyFromOp(Operation *op) {
6199 return llvm::TypeSwitch<Operation *, TargetDirectiveEnumTy>(op)
6200 .Case([](omp::TargetDataOp) { return TargetDirectiveEnumTy::TargetData; })
6201 .Case([](omp::TargetEnterDataOp) {
6202 return TargetDirectiveEnumTy::TargetEnterData;
6203 })
6204 .Case([&](omp::TargetExitDataOp) {
6205 return TargetDirectiveEnumTy::TargetExitData;
6206 })
6207 .Case([&](omp::TargetUpdateOp) {
6208 return TargetDirectiveEnumTy::TargetUpdate;
6209 })
6210 .Case([&](omp::TargetOp) { return TargetDirectiveEnumTy::Target; })
6211 .Default([&](Operation *op) { return TargetDirectiveEnumTy::None; });
6212}
6213
6214} // namespace
6215
6216static uint64_t getArrayElementSizeInBits(LLVM::LLVMArrayType arrTy,
6217 DataLayout &dl) {
6218 if (auto nestedArrTy = llvm::dyn_cast_if_present<LLVM::LLVMArrayType>(
6219 arrTy.getElementType()))
6220 return getArrayElementSizeInBits(nestedArrTy, dl);
6221 return dl.getTypeSizeInBits(arrTy.getElementType());
6222}
6223
6224// This function calculates the size to be offloaded for a specified type, given
6225// its associated map clause (which can contain bounds information which affects
6226// the total size), this size is calculated based on the underlying element type
6227// e.g. given a 1-D array of ints, we will calculate the size from the integer
6228// type * number of elements in the array. This size can be used in other
6229// calculations but is ultimately used as an argument to the OpenMP runtimes
6230// kernel argument structure which is generated through the combinedInfo data
6231// structures.
6232// This function is somewhat equivalent to Clang's getExprTypeSize inside of
6233// CGOpenMPRuntime.cpp.
6234static llvm::Value *getSizeInBytes(DataLayout &dl, const mlir::Type &type,
6235 Operation *clauseOp,
6236 llvm::Value *basePointer,
6237 llvm::Type *baseType,
6238 llvm::IRBuilderBase &builder,
6239 LLVM::ModuleTranslation &moduleTranslation) {
6240 if (auto memberClause =
6241 mlir::dyn_cast_if_present<mlir::omp::MapInfoOp>(clauseOp)) {
6242 // This calculates the size to transfer based on bounds and the underlying
6243 // element type, provided bounds have been specified (Fortran
6244 // pointers/allocatables/target and arrays that have sections specified fall
6245 // into this as well)
6246 if (!memberClause.getBounds().empty()) {
6247 llvm::Value *elementCount = builder.getInt64(1);
6248 for (auto bounds : memberClause.getBounds()) {
6249 if (auto boundOp = mlir::dyn_cast_if_present<mlir::omp::MapBoundsOp>(
6250 bounds.getDefiningOp())) {
6251 // The below calculation for the size to be mapped calculated from the
6252 // map.info's bounds is: (elemCount * [UB - LB] + 1), later we
6253 // multiply by the underlying element types byte size to get the full
6254 // size to be offloaded based on the bounds
6255 elementCount = builder.CreateMul(
6256 elementCount,
6257 builder.CreateAdd(
6258 builder.CreateSub(
6259 moduleTranslation.lookupValue(boundOp.getUpperBound()),
6260 moduleTranslation.lookupValue(boundOp.getLowerBound())),
6261 builder.getInt64(1)));
6262 }
6263 }
6264
6265 // utilising getTypeSizeInBits instead of getTypeSize as getTypeSize gives
6266 // the size in inconsistent byte or bit format.
6267 uint64_t underlyingTypeSzInBits = dl.getTypeSizeInBits(type);
6268 if (auto arrTy = llvm::dyn_cast_if_present<LLVM::LLVMArrayType>(type))
6269 underlyingTypeSzInBits = getArrayElementSizeInBits(arrTy, dl);
6270
6271 // The size in bytes x number of elements, the sizeInBytes stored is
6272 // the underyling types size, e.g. if ptr<i32>, it'll be the i32's
6273 // size, so we do some on the fly runtime math to get the size in
6274 // bytes from the extent (ub - lb) * sizeInBytes. NOTE: This may need
6275 // some adjustment for members with more complex types.
6276 return builder.CreateMul(elementCount,
6277 builder.getInt64(underlyingTypeSzInBits / 8));
6278 }
6279 }
6280
6281 return builder.getInt64(dl.getTypeSizeInBits(type) / 8);
6282}
6283
6284// Convert the MLIR map flag set to the runtime map flag set for embedding
6285// in LLVM-IR. This is important as the two bit-flag lists do not correspond
6286// 1-to-1 as there's flags the runtime doesn't care about and vice versa.
6287// Certain flags are discarded here such as RefPtee and co.
6288static llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags
6289convertClauseMapFlags(omp::ClauseMapFlags mlirFlags) {
6290 const bool hasExplicitMap =
6291 (mlirFlags & ~omp::ClauseMapFlags::is_device_ptr) !=
6292 omp::ClauseMapFlags::none;
6293
6294 llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags mapType =
6295 llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags::OMP_MAP_NONE;
6296
6297 if (bitEnumContainsAll(mlirFlags, omp::ClauseMapFlags::to))
6298 mapType |= llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags::OMP_MAP_TO;
6299
6300 if (bitEnumContainsAll(mlirFlags, omp::ClauseMapFlags::from))
6301 mapType |= llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags::OMP_MAP_FROM;
6302
6303 if (bitEnumContainsAll(mlirFlags, omp::ClauseMapFlags::always))
6304 mapType |= llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags::OMP_MAP_ALWAYS;
6305
6306 if (bitEnumContainsAll(mlirFlags, omp::ClauseMapFlags::del))
6307 mapType |= llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags::OMP_MAP_DELETE;
6308
6309 if (bitEnumContainsAll(mlirFlags, omp::ClauseMapFlags::return_param))
6310 mapType |= llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags::OMP_MAP_RETURN_PARAM;
6311
6312 if (bitEnumContainsAll(mlirFlags, omp::ClauseMapFlags::priv))
6313 mapType |= llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags::OMP_MAP_PRIVATE;
6314
6315 if (bitEnumContainsAll(mlirFlags, omp::ClauseMapFlags::literal))
6316 mapType |= llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags::OMP_MAP_LITERAL;
6317
6318 if (bitEnumContainsAll(mlirFlags, omp::ClauseMapFlags::implicit))
6319 mapType |= llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags::OMP_MAP_IMPLICIT;
6320
6321 if (bitEnumContainsAll(mlirFlags, omp::ClauseMapFlags::close))
6322 mapType |= llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags::OMP_MAP_CLOSE;
6323
6324 if (bitEnumContainsAll(mlirFlags, omp::ClauseMapFlags::present))
6325 mapType |= llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags::OMP_MAP_PRESENT;
6326
6327 if (bitEnumContainsAll(mlirFlags, omp::ClauseMapFlags::ompx_hold))
6328 mapType |= llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags::OMP_MAP_OMPX_HOLD;
6329
6330 if (bitEnumContainsAll(mlirFlags, omp::ClauseMapFlags::attach))
6331 mapType |= llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags::OMP_MAP_ATTACH;
6332
6333 if (bitEnumContainsAll(mlirFlags, omp::ClauseMapFlags::is_device_ptr)) {
6334 mapType |= llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags::OMP_MAP_TARGET_PARAM;
6335 if (!hasExplicitMap)
6336 mapType |= llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags::OMP_MAP_LITERAL;
6337 }
6338
6339 return mapType;
6340}
6341
6342static void collectMapDataFromMapOperands(
6343 MapInfoData &mapData, SmallVectorImpl<Value> &mapVars,
6344 LLVM::ModuleTranslation &moduleTranslation, DataLayout &dl,
6345 llvm::IRBuilderBase &builder, ArrayRef<Value> useDevPtrOperands = {},
6346 ArrayRef<Value> useDevAddrOperands = {},
6347 ArrayRef<Value> hasDevAddrOperands = {}) {
6348
6349 auto checkRefPtrOrPteeMapWithAttach = [](omp::ClauseMapFlags mapType) {
6350 bool hasRefType =
6351 bitEnumContainsAll(mapType, omp::ClauseMapFlags::ref_ptr) ||
6352 bitEnumContainsAll(mapType, omp::ClauseMapFlags::ref_ptee);
6353 return hasRefType &&
6354 bitEnumContainsAll(mapType, omp::ClauseMapFlags::attach);
6355 };
6356
6357 auto checkIsAMember = [](const auto &mapVars, auto mapOp) {
6358 // Check if this is a member mapping and correctly assign that it is, if
6359 // it is a member of a larger object.
6360 // TODO: Need better handling of members, and distinguishing of members
6361 // that are implicitly allocated on device vs explicitly passed in as
6362 // arguments.
6363 // TODO: May require some further additions to support nested record
6364 // types, i.e. member maps that can have member maps.
6365 for (Value mapValue : mapVars) {
6366 auto map = cast<omp::MapInfoOp>(mapValue.getDefiningOp());
6367 for (auto member : map.getMembers())
6368 if (member == mapOp)
6369 return true;
6370 }
6371 return false;
6372 };
6373
6374 // Process MapOperands
6375 for (Value mapValue : mapVars) {
6376 auto mapOp = cast<omp::MapInfoOp>(mapValue.getDefiningOp());
6377 bool isRefPtrOrPteeMapWithAttach =
6378 checkRefPtrOrPteeMapWithAttach(mapOp.getMapType());
6379 Value offloadPtr = (mapOp.getVarPtrPtr() && !isRefPtrOrPteeMapWithAttach)
6380 ? mapOp.getVarPtrPtr()
6381 : mapOp.getVarPtr();
6382 mapData.OriginalValue.push_back(moduleTranslation.lookupValue(offloadPtr));
6383 mapData.Pointers.push_back(
6384 isRefPtrOrPteeMapWithAttach
6385 ? moduleTranslation.lookupValue(mapOp.getVarPtrPtr())
6386 : mapData.OriginalValue.back());
6387
6388 if (llvm::Value *refPtr =
6389 getRefPtrIfDeclareTarget(offloadPtr, moduleTranslation)) {
6390 mapData.IsDeclareTarget.push_back(true);
6391 mapData.BasePointers.push_back(refPtr);
6392 } else if (isDeclareTargetTo(offloadPtr)) {
6393 mapData.IsDeclareTarget.push_back(true);
6394 mapData.BasePointers.push_back(mapData.OriginalValue.back());
6395 } else { // regular mapped variable
6396 mapData.IsDeclareTarget.push_back(false);
6397 mapData.BasePointers.push_back(mapData.OriginalValue.back());
6398 }
6399
6400 // In every situation we currently have if we have a varPtrPtr present
6401 // we wish to utilise it's type for the base type, main cases are
6402 // currently Fortran descriptor base address maps and attach maps.
6403 mapData.BaseType.push_back(moduleTranslation.convertType(
6404 mapOp.getVarPtrPtr() ? mapOp.getVarPtrPtrType().value()
6405 : mapOp.getVarPtrType()));
6406
6407 // For the attach map cases, it's a little odd, as we effectively have to
6408 // utilise the base address (including all bounds offsets) for the pointer
6409 // field, the pointer address for the base address field, and the pointer
6410 // not the data (base addresses) size. So we end up with a mix of base
6411 // types and sizes we wish to insert here.
6412 mlir::Type sizeType = (isRefPtrOrPteeMapWithAttach || !mapOp.getVarPtrPtr())
6413 ? mapOp.getVarPtrType()
6414 : mapOp.getVarPtrPtrType().value();
6415 mapData.Sizes.push_back(getSizeInBytes(
6416 dl, sizeType, isRefPtrOrPteeMapWithAttach ? nullptr : mapOp,
6417 mapData.Pointers.back(), moduleTranslation.convertType(sizeType),
6418 builder, moduleTranslation));
6419 mapData.MapClause.push_back(mapOp.getOperation());
6420 mapData.Types.push_back(convertClauseMapFlags(mapOp.getMapType()));
6421 mapData.Names.push_back(LLVM::createMappingInformation(
6422 mapOp.getLoc(), *moduleTranslation.getOpenMPBuilder()));
6423 mapData.DevicePointers.push_back(llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::DeviceInfoTy::None);
6424 if (mapOp.getMapperId())
6425 mapData.Mappers.push_back(
6426 SymbolTable::lookupNearestSymbolFrom<omp::DeclareMapperOp>(
6427 mapOp, mapOp.getMapperIdAttr()));
6428 else
6429 mapData.Mappers.push_back(nullptr);
6430 mapData.IsAMapping.push_back(true);
6431 mapData.IsAMember.push_back(checkIsAMember(mapVars, mapOp));
6432 }
6433
6434 auto findMapInfo = [&mapData](llvm::Value *val,
6435 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::DeviceInfoTy devInfoTy,
6436 size_t memberCount) {
6437 unsigned index = 0;
6438 bool found = false;
6439 for (llvm::Value *basePtr : mapData.OriginalValue) {
6440 auto mapOp = cast<omp::MapInfoOp>(mapData.MapClause[index]);
6441 // TODO: Currently we define an equivalent mapping as
6442 // the same base pointer and an equivalent member count, but
6443 // that is a loose definition. We may have to extend to check
6444 // for other fields (varPtrPtr/individual members being mapped).
6445 // Note: Attach maps are not the same as a normal data transfer
6446 // they specify to the runtime to perform an attach map and they
6447 // (at least at the moment) are never something we would aim to
6448 // return in a use_dev_* clause, so they are skipped in terms of
6449 // duplicate maps.
6450 bool isAttachMap =
6451 (mapData.Types[index] &
6452 llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags::OMP_MAP_ATTACH) ==
6453 llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags::OMP_MAP_ATTACH;
6454 if (!isAttachMap && basePtr == val && mapData.IsAMapping[index] &&
6455 memberCount == mapOp.getMembers().size()) {
6456 found = true;
6457 mapData.Types[index] |=
6458 llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags::OMP_MAP_RETURN_PARAM;
6459 mapData.DevicePointers[index] = devInfoTy;
6460 }
6461 index++;
6462 }
6463 return found;
6464 };
6465
6466 // Process useDevPtr(Addr)Operands
6467 auto addDevInfos = [&](const llvm::ArrayRef<Value> &useDevOperands,
6468 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::DeviceInfoTy devInfoTy) {
6469 for (Value mapValue : useDevOperands) {
6470 auto mapOp = cast<omp::MapInfoOp>(mapValue.getDefiningOp());
6471 Value offloadPtr =
6472 mapOp.getVarPtrPtr() ? mapOp.getVarPtrPtr() : mapOp.getVarPtr();
6473 llvm::Value *origValue = moduleTranslation.lookupValue(offloadPtr);
6474
6475 // Check if map info is already present for this entry.
6476 if (!findMapInfo(origValue, devInfoTy, mapOp.getMembers().size())) {
6477 mapData.OriginalValue.push_back(origValue);
6478 mapData.Pointers.push_back(mapData.OriginalValue.back());
6479 mapData.IsDeclareTarget.push_back(false);
6480 mapData.BasePointers.push_back(mapData.OriginalValue.back());
6481 mlir::Type baseTy = mapOp.getVarPtrPtr()
6482 ? mapOp.getVarPtrPtrType().value()
6483 : mapOp.getVarPtrType();
6484 mapData.BaseType.push_back(moduleTranslation.convertType(baseTy));
6485 mapData.Sizes.push_back(builder.getInt64(0));
6486 mapData.MapClause.push_back(mapOp.getOperation());
6487 mapData.Types.push_back(
6488 llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags::OMP_MAP_RETURN_PARAM);
6489 mapData.Names.push_back(LLVM::createMappingInformation(
6490 mapOp.getLoc(), *moduleTranslation.getOpenMPBuilder()));
6491 mapData.DevicePointers.push_back(devInfoTy);
6492 mapData.Mappers.push_back(nullptr);
6493 mapData.IsAMapping.push_back(false);
6494 mapData.IsAMember.push_back(checkIsAMember(useDevOperands, mapOp));
6495 }
6496 }
6497 };
6498
6499 addDevInfos(useDevAddrOperands, llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::DeviceInfoTy::Address);
6500 addDevInfos(useDevPtrOperands, llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::DeviceInfoTy::Pointer);
6501
6502 for (Value mapValue : hasDevAddrOperands) {
6503 auto mapOp = cast<omp::MapInfoOp>(mapValue.getDefiningOp());
6504 Value offloadPtr =
6505 mapOp.getVarPtrPtr() ? mapOp.getVarPtrPtr() : mapOp.getVarPtr();
6506 llvm::Value *origValue = moduleTranslation.lookupValue(offloadPtr);
6507 auto mapType = convertClauseMapFlags(mapOp.getMapType());
6508 auto mapTypeAlways = llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags::OMP_MAP_ALWAYS;
6509 bool isDevicePtr =
6510 (mapOp.getMapType() & omp::ClauseMapFlags::is_device_ptr) !=
6511 omp::ClauseMapFlags::none;
6512
6513 mapData.OriginalValue.push_back(origValue);
6514 mapData.BasePointers.push_back(origValue);
6515 mapData.Pointers.push_back(origValue);
6516 mapData.IsDeclareTarget.push_back(false);
6517
6518 mlir::Type baseTy = mapOp.getVarPtrPtr() ? mapOp.getVarPtrPtrType().value()
6519 : mapOp.getVarPtrType();
6520 mapData.BaseType.push_back(moduleTranslation.convertType(baseTy));
6521 mapData.Sizes.push_back(builder.getInt64(dl.getTypeSize(baseTy)));
6522
6523 mapData.MapClause.push_back(mapOp.getOperation());
6524 if (llvm::to_underlying(mapType & mapTypeAlways)) {
6525 // Descriptors are mapped with the ALWAYS flag, since they can get
6526 // rematerialized, so the address of the decriptor for a given object
6527 // may change from one place to another.
6528 mapData.Types.push_back(mapType);
6529 // Technically it's possible for a non-descriptor mapping to have
6530 // both has-device-addr and ALWAYS, so lookup the mapper in case it
6531 // exists.
6532 if (mapOp.getMapperId()) {
6533 mapData.Mappers.push_back(
6534 SymbolTable::lookupNearestSymbolFrom<omp::DeclareMapperOp>(
6535 mapOp, mapOp.getMapperIdAttr()));
6536 } else {
6537 mapData.Mappers.push_back(nullptr);
6538 }
6539 } else {
6540 // For is_device_ptr we need the map type to propagate so the runtime
6541 // can materialize the device-side copy of the pointer container.
6542 mapData.Types.push_back(
6543 isDevicePtr ? mapType
6544 : llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags::OMP_MAP_LITERAL);
6545 mapData.Mappers.push_back(nullptr);
6546 }
6547 mapData.Names.push_back(LLVM::createMappingInformation(
6548 mapOp.getLoc(), *moduleTranslation.getOpenMPBuilder()));
6549 mapData.DevicePointers.push_back(
6550 isDevicePtr ? llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::DeviceInfoTy::Pointer
6551 : llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::DeviceInfoTy::Address);
6552 mapData.IsAMapping.push_back(false);
6553 mapData.IsAMember.push_back(checkIsAMember(hasDevAddrOperands, mapOp));
6554 }
6555}
6556
6557static int getMapDataMemberIdx(MapInfoData &mapData, omp::MapInfoOp memberOp) {
6558 auto *res = llvm::find(mapData.MapClause, memberOp);
6559 assert(res != mapData.MapClause.end() &&
6560 "MapInfoOp for member not found in MapData, cannot return index");
6561 return std::distance(mapData.MapClause.begin(), res);
6562}
6563
6564static void sortMapIndices(llvm::SmallVectorImpl<size_t> &indices,
6565 omp::MapInfoOp mapInfo, bool first = true) {
6566 ArrayAttr indexAttr = mapInfo.getMembersIndexAttr();
6567 llvm::SmallVector<size_t> occludedChildren;
6568 llvm::sort(
6569 indices.begin(), indices.end(), [&](const size_t a, const size_t b) {
6570 // Bail early if we are asked to look at the same index. If we do not
6571 // bail early, we can end up mistakenly adding indices to
6572 // occludedChildren. This can occur with some types of libc++ hardening.
6573 if (a == b)
6574 return false;
6575
6576 auto memberIndicesA = cast<ArrayAttr>(indexAttr[a]);
6577 auto memberIndicesB = cast<ArrayAttr>(indexAttr[b]);
6578
6579 for (auto it : llvm::zip(memberIndicesA, memberIndicesB)) {
6580 int64_t aIndex = mlir::cast<IntegerAttr>(std::get<0>(it)).getInt();
6581 int64_t bIndex = mlir::cast<IntegerAttr>(std::get<1>(it)).getInt();
6582
6583 if (aIndex == bIndex)
6584 continue;
6585
6586 if (aIndex < bIndex)
6587 return first;
6588
6589 if (aIndex > bIndex)
6590 return !first;
6591 }
6592
6593 // Iterated up until the end of the smallest member and
6594 // they were found to be equal up to that point, so select
6595 // the member with the lowest index count, so the "parent"
6596 bool memberAParent = memberIndicesA.size() < memberIndicesB.size();
6597 if (memberAParent)
6598 occludedChildren.push_back(b);
6599 else
6600 occludedChildren.push_back(a);
6601 return memberAParent;
6602 });
6603
6604 for (auto v : occludedChildren)
6605 indices.erase(std::remove(indices.begin(), indices.end(), v),
6606 indices.end());
6607}
6608
6609static omp::MapInfoOp getFirstOrLastMappedMemberPtr(omp::MapInfoOp mapInfo,
6610 bool first) {
6611 ArrayAttr indexAttr = mapInfo.getMembersIndexAttr();
6612 // Only 1 member has been mapped, we can return it.
6613 if (indexAttr.size() == 1)
6614 return cast<omp::MapInfoOp>(mapInfo.getMembers()[0].getDefiningOp());
6615 llvm::SmallVector<size_t> indices(indexAttr.size());
6616 std::iota(indices.begin(), indices.end(), 0);
6617 sortMapIndices(indices, mapInfo, first);
6618 return llvm::cast<omp::MapInfoOp>(
6619 mapInfo.getMembers()[indices.front()].getDefiningOp());
6620}
6621
6622/// This function calculates the array/pointer offset for map data provided
6623/// with bounds operations, e.g. when provided something like the following:
6624///
6625/// Fortran
6626/// map(tofrom: array(2:5, 3:2))
6627///
6628/// We must calculate the initial pointer offset to pass across, this function
6629/// performs this using bounds.
6630///
6631/// TODO/WARNING: This only supports Fortran's column major indexing currently
6632/// as is noted in the note below and comments in the function, we must extend
6633/// this function when we add a C++ frontend.
6634/// NOTE: which while specified in row-major order it currently needs to be
6635/// flipped for Fortran's column order array allocation and access (as
6636/// opposed to C++'s row-major, hence the backwards processing where order is
6637/// important). This is likely important to keep in mind for the future when
6638/// we incorporate a C++ frontend, both frontends will need to agree on the
6639/// ordering of generated bounds operations (one may have to flip them) to
6640/// make the below lowering frontend agnostic. The offload size
6641/// calcualtion may also have to be adjusted for C++.
6642static std::vector<llvm::Value *>
6643calculateBoundsOffset(LLVM::ModuleTranslation &moduleTranslation,
6644 llvm::IRBuilderBase &builder, bool isArrayTy,
6645 OperandRange bounds) {
6646 std::vector<llvm::Value *> idx;
6647 // There's no bounds to calculate an offset from, we can safely
6648 // ignore and return no indices.
6649 if (bounds.empty())
6650 return idx;
6651
6652 // If we have an array type, then we have its type so can treat it as a
6653 // normal GEP instruction where the bounds operations are simply indexes
6654 // into the array. We currently do reverse order of the bounds, which
6655 // I believe leans more towards Fortran's column-major in memory.
6656 if (isArrayTy) {
6657 idx.push_back(builder.getInt64(0));
6658 for (int i = bounds.size() - 1; i >= 0; --i) {
6659 if (auto boundOp = dyn_cast_if_present<omp::MapBoundsOp>(
6660 bounds[i].getDefiningOp())) {
6661 idx.push_back(moduleTranslation.lookupValue(boundOp.getLowerBound()));
6662 }
6663 }
6664 } else {
6665 // If we do not have an array type, but we have bounds, then we're dealing
6666 // with a pointer that's being treated like an array and we have the
6667 // underlying type e.g. an i32, or f64 etc, e.g. a fortran descriptor base
6668 // address (pointer pointing to the actual data) so we must caclulate the
6669 // offset using a single index which the following loop attempts to
6670 // compute using the standard column-major algorithm e.g for a 3D array:
6671 //
6672 // ((((c_idx * b_len) + b_idx) * a_len) + a_idx)
6673 //
6674 // It is of note that it's doing column-major rather than row-major at the
6675 // moment, but having a way for the frontend to indicate which major format
6676 // to use or standardizing/canonicalizing the order of the bounds to compute
6677 // the offset may be useful in the future when there's other frontends with
6678 // different formats.
6679 for (int i = bounds.size() - 1; i >= 0; --i) {
6680 if (auto boundOp = dyn_cast_if_present<omp::MapBoundsOp>(
6681 bounds[i].getDefiningOp())) {
6682 if (i == ((int)bounds.size() - 1))
6683 idx.emplace_back(
6684 moduleTranslation.lookupValue(boundOp.getLowerBound()));
6685 else
6686 idx.back() = builder.CreateAdd(
6687 builder.CreateMul(idx.back(), moduleTranslation.lookupValue(
6688 boundOp.getExtent())),
6689 moduleTranslation.lookupValue(boundOp.getLowerBound()));
6690 }
6691 }
6692 }
6693
6694 return idx;
6695}
6696
6697static void getAsIntegers(ArrayAttr values, llvm::SmallVector<int64_t> &ints) {
6698 llvm::transform(values, std::back_inserter(ints), [](Attribute value) {
6699 return cast<IntegerAttr>(value).getInt();
6700 });
6701}
6702
6703// Gathers members that are overlapping in the parent, excluding members that
6704// themselves overlap, keeping the top-most (closest to parents level) map.
6705static void
6706getOverlappedMembers(llvm::SmallVectorImpl<size_t> &overlapMapDataIdxs,
6707 omp::MapInfoOp parentOp) {
6708 // No members mapped, no overlaps.
6709 if (parentOp.getMembers().empty())
6710 return;
6711
6712 // Single member, we can insert and return early.
6713 if (parentOp.getMembers().size() == 1) {
6714 overlapMapDataIdxs.push_back(0);
6715 return;
6716 }
6717
6718 ArrayAttr indexAttr = parentOp.getMembersIndexAttr();
6719 size_t numMembers = indexAttr.size();
6720
6721 // Pre-convert all member indices to integer arrays for efficient comparison.
6722 llvm::SmallVector<llvm::SmallVector<int64_t>> memberIndices(numMembers);
6723 for (auto [i, indicesAttr] : llvm::enumerate(indexAttr))
6724 getAsIntegers(cast<ArrayAttr>(indicesAttr), memberIndices[i]);
6725
6726 // For each member, check if it's superseded by another (shorter prefix)
6727 // member. If member j's indices are a prefix of member i's indices, then
6728 // i is a child of j and should be skipped. e.g. if member [0] is mapped,
6729 // we skip members [0,1], [0,2], etc.
6730 llvm::SmallDenseSet<size_t> skipIndices;
6731 for (size_t i = 0; i < numMembers; ++i) {
6732 const auto &iIndices = memberIndices[i];
6733 for (size_t j = 0; j < numMembers; ++j) {
6734 if (i == j)
6735 continue;
6736 const auto &jIndices = memberIndices[j];
6737 // If j's indices are a strict prefix of i's indices, skip i
6738 if (jIndices.size() < iIndices.size() &&
6739 std::equal(jIndices.begin(), jIndices.end(), iIndices.begin())) {
6740 skipIndices.insert(i);
6741 break; // No need to check other potential parents
6742 }
6743 }
6744 }
6745
6746 // Collect indices of members that are not superseded by a parent.
6747 for (size_t i = 0; i < numMembers; ++i)
6748 if (!skipIndices.contains(i))
6749 overlapMapDataIdxs.push_back(i);
6750}
6751
6752// The intent is to verify if the mapped data being passed is a
6753// pointer -> pointee that requires special handling in certain cases,
6754// e.g. applying the OMP_MAP_PTR_AND_OBJ map type.
6755//
6756// There may be a better way to verify this, but unfortunately with
6757// opaque pointers we lose the ability to easily check if something is
6758// a pointer whilst maintaining access to the underlying type.
6759static bool checkIfPointerMap(omp::MapInfoOp mapOp) {
6760 // If we have a varPtrPtr field assigned then the underlying type is a pointer
6761 if (mapOp.getVarPtrPtr())
6762 return true;
6763
6764 // If the map data is declare target with a link clause, then it's represented
6765 // as a pointer when we lower it to LLVM-IR even if at the MLIR level it has
6766 // no relation to pointers.
6767 if (isDeclareTargetLink(mapOp.getVarPtr()))
6768 return true;
6769
6770 return false;
6771}
6772
6773/// This function handles the insertion of a single item of map data from
6774/// MapInfoData into the OMPIRBuilder's MapInfo list. Utilising this function
6775/// means the map being inserted can be treated as a non-parent map entity,
6776/// if the memberOfFlag is set then the map being inserted is treated as
6777/// a member map of a larger entity. The insertion into the MapInfo list of
6778/// the OMPIRBuilder can vary based on a number of factors, such as if it's
6779/// a ref_ptr or ref_ptee map, if it's a member of a record, what construct
6780/// the map belongs to and the various map type bit flags that are set for
6781/// the map.
6782static void
6783processIndividualMap(llvm::IRBuilderBase &builder,
6784 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder &ompBuilder, MapInfoData &mapData,
6785 size_t mapDataIdx, MapInfosTy &combinedInfo,
6786 TargetDirectiveEnumTy targetDirective,
6787 llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags memberOfFlag =
6788 llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags::OMP_MAP_NONE,
6789 bool isTargetParam = true, int mapDataParentIdx = -1) {
6790 auto mapFlag = mapData.Types[mapDataIdx];
6791 auto mapInfoOp = llvm::cast<omp::MapInfoOp>(mapData.MapClause[mapDataIdx]);
6792
6793 bool isPtrTy = checkIfPointerMap(mapInfoOp);
6794 bool isAttachMap = ((convertClauseMapFlags(mapInfoOp.getMapType()) &
6795 llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags::OMP_MAP_ATTACH) ==
6796 llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags::OMP_MAP_ATTACH);
6797
6798 // Declare target variables are not passed to the kernel, and for the moment
6799 // attach maps are not passed to the kernel. However, it is possible to create
6800 // attach maps that transfer data and thus can be kernel arguments, but our
6801 // existing frontend does not do this.
6802 if (isTargetParam &&
6803 (targetDirective == TargetDirectiveEnumTy::Target &&
6804 !mapData.IsDeclareTarget[mapDataIdx]) &&
6805 !isAttachMap)
6806 mapFlag |= llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags::OMP_MAP_TARGET_PARAM;
6807
6808 if (mapInfoOp.getMapCaptureType() == omp::VariableCaptureKind::ByCopy &&
6809 !isPtrTy)
6810 mapFlag |= llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags::OMP_MAP_LITERAL;
6811
6812 // If we have a pointer and it's part of a MEMBER_OF mapping we do not apply
6813 // MEMBER_OF, as the runtime currently has a work-around that utilises
6814 // MEMBER_OF to prevent reference updating in certain scenarios instead of
6815 // target_param. However, this causes a noticeable issue in cases where we
6816 // map some data (Fortran descriptor primarily at the moment), alter it on
6817 // the host, and then expect it to not be updated in a subsequent implicit map
6818 // (such as an implicit map on a target).
6819 if (memberOfFlag != llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags::OMP_MAP_NONE) {
6820 if (!isPtrTy && !isAttachMap)
6821 ompBuilder.setCorrectMemberOfFlag(mapFlag, memberOfFlag);
6822
6823 // The return parameter should be the over-riding parent in cases where we
6824 // have a return parameter that is echoed to all members, the main case of
6825 // this currently is with fortran descriptors. It may need more finessing
6826 // for C/C++ in the future or descriptors that are members of derived
6827 // types.
6828 mapFlag &= ~llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags::OMP_MAP_RETURN_PARAM;
6829 }
6830
6831 // We apply MAP_PTR_AND_OBJ when within a declare mapper object as it enforces
6832 // MEMBER_OF mappings on maps that are passed the initial nesting depth, which
6833 // includes pointed to data and attach members, both of which are technically
6834 // not part of the main object. This has the side effect of causing early
6835 // map-backs in certain cases where an implicit declare mapper has been
6836 // emitted for a target region. Applying MAP_PTR_AND_OBJ in these situations
6837 // circumvents this.
6838 if (isPtrTy && !isAttachMap && mapData.IsDeclareTarget[mapDataIdx])
6839 mapFlag |= llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags::OMP_MAP_PTR_AND_OBJ;
6840
6841 // if we're provided a mapDataParentIdx, then the data being mapped is
6842 // part of a larger object (in a parent <-> member mapping) and in this
6843 // case our BasePointer should be the parent. Except in the edge case
6844 // where we are mapping pointee data, where we try staying close to
6845 // what Clang currently does and utilise the regular base pointer of the
6846 // data.
6847 bool isRefPtee =
6848 !bitEnumContainsAll(mapInfoOp.getMapType(),
6849 omp::ClauseMapFlags::ref_ptr) &&
6850 bitEnumContainsAll(mapInfoOp.getMapType(), omp::ClauseMapFlags::ref_ptee);
6851 bool isRefPtrPtee = bitEnumContainsAll(mapInfoOp.getMapType(),
6852 omp::ClauseMapFlags::ref_ptr |
6853 omp::ClauseMapFlags::ref_ptee);
6854
6855 if (!mapInfoOp->getParentOfType<omp::DeclareMapperOp>() &&
6856 mapDataParentIdx >= 0 && !(isRefPtee || (isRefPtrPtee && isPtrTy))) {
6857 combinedInfo.BasePointers.emplace_back(
6858 mapData.BasePointers[mapDataParentIdx]);
6859 } else {
6860 combinedInfo.BasePointers.emplace_back(mapData.BasePointers[mapDataIdx]);
6861 }
6862
6863 combinedInfo.Pointers.emplace_back(mapData.Pointers[mapDataIdx]);
6864 combinedInfo.DevicePointers.emplace_back(
6865 memberOfFlag != llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags::OMP_MAP_NONE
6866 ? llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::DeviceInfoTy::None
6867 : mapData.DevicePointers[mapDataIdx]);
6868 combinedInfo.Mappers.emplace_back(mapData.Mappers[mapDataIdx]);
6869 combinedInfo.Names.emplace_back(mapData.Names[mapDataIdx]);
6870 combinedInfo.Types.emplace_back(mapFlag);
6871 combinedInfo.Sizes.emplace_back(
6872 isPtrTy ? builder.CreateSelect(
6873 builder.CreateIsNull(mapData.Pointers[mapDataIdx]),
6874 builder.getInt64(0), mapData.Sizes[mapDataIdx])
6875 : mapData.Sizes[mapDataIdx]);
6876}
6877
6878// This creates two insertions into the MapInfosTy data structure for the
6879// "parent" of a set of members, (usually a container e.g.
6880// class/structure/derived type) when subsequent members have also been
6881// explicitly mapped on the same map clause. Certain types, such as Fortran
6882// descriptors are mapped like this as well, however, the members are
6883// implicit as far as a user is concerned, but we must explicitly map them
6884// internally.
6885//
6886// This function also returns the memberOfFlag for this particular parent,
6887// which is utilised in subsequent member mappings (by modifying there map type
6888// with it) to indicate that a member is part of this parent and should be
6889// treated by the runtime as such. Important to achieve the correct mapping.
6890//
6891// This function borrows a lot from Clang's emitCombinedEntry function
6892// inside of CGOpenMPRuntime.cpp
6893static void mapParentWithMembers(
6894 LLVM::ModuleTranslation &moduleTranslation, llvm::IRBuilderBase &builder,
6895 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder &ompBuilder, DataLayout &dl, MapInfosTy &combinedInfo,
6896 MapInfoData &mapData, uint64_t mapDataIndex,
6897 llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags memberOfFlag,
6898 TargetDirectiveEnumTy targetDirective) {
6899 using MapFlags = llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags;
6900 assert(!ompBuilder.Config.isTargetDevice() &&
6901 "function only supported for host device codegen");
6902 auto parentClause =
6903 llvm::cast<omp::MapInfoOp>(mapData.MapClause[mapDataIndex]);
6904 auto *parentMapper = mapData.Mappers[mapDataIndex];
6905
6906 // Map the first segment of the parent. If a user-defined mapper is attached,
6907 // include the parent's to/from-style bits (and common modifiers) in this
6908 // base entry so the mapper receives correct copy semantics via its 'type'
6909 // parameter. Also keep TARGET_PARAM when required for kernel arguments.
6910 MapFlags baseFlag = (targetDirective == TargetDirectiveEnumTy::Target &&
6911 !mapData.IsDeclareTarget[mapDataIndex])
6912 ? MapFlags::OMP_MAP_TARGET_PARAM
6913 : MapFlags::OMP_MAP_NONE;
6914
6915 if (parentMapper) {
6916 // Preserve relevant map-type bits from the parent clause. These include
6917 // the copy direction (TO/FROM), as well as commonly used modifiers that
6918 // should be visible to the mapper for correct behaviour.
6919 MapFlags parentFlags = mapData.Types[mapDataIndex];
6920 MapFlags preserve = MapFlags::OMP_MAP_TO | MapFlags::OMP_MAP_FROM |
6921 MapFlags::OMP_MAP_ALWAYS | MapFlags::OMP_MAP_CLOSE |
6922 MapFlags::OMP_MAP_PRESENT |
6923 MapFlags::OMP_MAP_OMPX_HOLD |
6924 MapFlags::OMP_MAP_IMPLICIT;
6925 baseFlag |= (parentFlags & preserve);
6926 } else {
6927 MapFlags parentFlags = mapData.Types[mapDataIndex];
6928 MapFlags preserve =
6929 MapFlags::OMP_MAP_PRESENT | MapFlags::OMP_MAP_RETURN_PARAM;
6930 baseFlag |= (parentFlags & preserve);
6931 }
6932
6933 combinedInfo.Types.emplace_back(baseFlag);
6934 combinedInfo.DevicePointers.emplace_back(
6935 mapData.DevicePointers[mapDataIndex]);
6936 // Only attach the mapper to the base entry when we are mapping the whole
6937 // parent. Combined/segment entries must not carry a mapper; otherwise the
6938 // mapper can be invoked with a partial size, which is undefined behaviour.
6939 combinedInfo.Mappers.emplace_back(
6940 parentMapper && !parentClause.getPartialMap() ? parentMapper : nullptr);
6941 combinedInfo.Names.emplace_back(LLVM::createMappingInformation(
6942 mapData.MapClause[mapDataIndex]->getLoc(), ompBuilder));
6943 combinedInfo.BasePointers.emplace_back(mapData.BasePointers[mapDataIndex]);
6944
6945 // Calculate size of the parent object being mapped based on the
6946 // addresses at runtime, highAddr - lowAddr = size. This of course
6947 // doesn't factor in allocated data like pointers, hence the further
6948 // processing of members specified by users, or in the case of
6949 // Fortran pointers and allocatables, the mapping of the pointed to
6950 // data by the descriptor (which itself, is a structure containing
6951 // runtime information on the dynamically allocated data).
6952 llvm::Value *lowAddr, *highAddr;
6953 if (!parentClause.getPartialMap()) {
6954 lowAddr = builder.CreatePointerCast(mapData.Pointers[mapDataIndex],
6955 builder.getPtrTy());
6956 highAddr = builder.CreatePointerCast(
6957 builder.CreateConstGEP1_32(mapData.BaseType[mapDataIndex],
6958 mapData.Pointers[mapDataIndex], 1),
6959 builder.getPtrTy());
6960 combinedInfo.Pointers.emplace_back(mapData.Pointers[mapDataIndex]);
6961 } else {
6962 auto mapOp = dyn_cast<omp::MapInfoOp>(mapData.MapClause[mapDataIndex]);
6963 int firstMemberIdx = getMapDataMemberIdx(
6964 mapData, getFirstOrLastMappedMemberPtr(mapOp, true));
6965 lowAddr = builder.CreatePointerCast(mapData.BasePointers[firstMemberIdx],
6966 builder.getPtrTy());
6967
6968 int lastMemberIdx = getMapDataMemberIdx(
6969 mapData, getFirstOrLastMappedMemberPtr(mapOp, false));
6970 auto lastMemberMapInfo =
6971 cast<omp::MapInfoOp>(mapData.MapClause[lastMemberIdx]);
6972
6973 // NOTE: Currently, for RefPtee the BaseType is set to the varPtrPtr field,
6974 // which is the pointer datas type and not the member within the structure
6975 // that it's part of, so we have to make sure we use the member type in this
6976 // case when calculating the parents size offsets.
6977 // TODO: May be good to extend MapInfoData to support tracking of both
6978 // VarPtr/VarPtrPtr BaseType's to better distinguish what's being used more
6979 // consistently.
6980 bool isRefPteeMap = bitEnumContainsAll(lastMemberMapInfo.getMapType(),
6981 omp::ClauseMapFlags::ref_ptee) &&
6982 !bitEnumContainsAll(lastMemberMapInfo.getMapType(),
6983 omp::ClauseMapFlags::ref_ptr);
6984 llvm::Type *castType = mapData.BaseType[lastMemberIdx];
6985 if (isRefPteeMap)
6986 castType =
6987 moduleTranslation.convertType(lastMemberMapInfo.getVarPtrType());
6988 highAddr = builder.CreatePointerCast(
6989 builder.CreateGEP(castType, mapData.BasePointers[lastMemberIdx],
6990 builder.getInt64(1)),
6991 builder.getPtrTy());
6992 combinedInfo.Pointers.emplace_back(mapData.BasePointers[firstMemberIdx]);
6993 }
6994
6995 llvm::Value *size = builder.CreateIntCast(
6996 builder.CreatePtrDiff(builder.getInt8Ty(), highAddr, lowAddr),
6997 builder.getInt64Ty(),
6998 /*isSigned=*/false);
6999 combinedInfo.Sizes.push_back(size);
7000
7001 // This creates the initial MEMBER_OF mapping that consists of
7002 // the parent/top level container (same as above effectively, except
7003 // with a fixed initial compile time size and separate maptype which
7004 // indicates the true mape type (tofrom etc.). This parent mapping is
7005 // only relevant if the structure in its totality is being mapped,
7006 // otherwise the above suffices.
7007 if (!parentClause.getPartialMap()) {
7008 // TODO: This will need to be expanded to include the whole host of logic
7009 // for the map flags that Clang currently supports (e.g. it should do some
7010 // further case specific flag modifications). For the moment, it handles
7011 // what we support as expected.
7012 MapFlags mapFlag = mapData.Types[mapDataIndex];
7013 bool hasMapClose = (MapFlags(mapFlag) & MapFlags::OMP_MAP_CLOSE) ==
7014 MapFlags::OMP_MAP_CLOSE;
7015 ompBuilder.setCorrectMemberOfFlag(mapFlag, memberOfFlag);
7016
7017 llvm::SmallVector<size_t> overlapIdxs;
7018 // Find all of the members that "overlap", i.e. occlude other members that
7019 // were mapped alongside the parent, e.g. member [0], occludes [0,1] and
7020 // [0,2], but not [1,0].
7021 getOverlappedMembers(overlapIdxs, parentClause);
7022
7023 // When we only have one overlap we skip the case that tries to segment the
7024 // mapping as best it can without creating holes, as the calculation is more
7025 // likely to have more overhead than anything we gain from mapping a smaller
7026 // chunk of data. This can be seen in cases where we are mapping Fortran
7027 // descriptors which are a special case of record type mapping.
7028 //
7029 // The cases for close and update are unique edge cases where the segmenting
7030 // does not play well with the runtime currently.
7031 if (targetDirective == TargetDirectiveEnumTy::TargetUpdate || hasMapClose ||
7032 overlapIdxs.size() == 1) {
7033 combinedInfo.Types.emplace_back(mapFlag);
7034 combinedInfo.DevicePointers.emplace_back(
7035 mapData.DevicePointers[mapDataIndex]);
7036 combinedInfo.Names.emplace_back(LLVM::createMappingInformation(
7037 mapData.MapClause[mapDataIndex]->getLoc(), ompBuilder));
7038 combinedInfo.BasePointers.emplace_back(
7039 mapData.BasePointers[mapDataIndex]);
7040 combinedInfo.Pointers.emplace_back(mapData.Pointers[mapDataIndex]);
7041 combinedInfo.Sizes.emplace_back(mapData.Sizes[mapDataIndex]);
7042 combinedInfo.Mappers.emplace_back(nullptr);
7043 } else {
7044 // We need to make sure the overlapped members are sorted in order of
7045 // lowest address to highest address.
7046 sortMapIndices(overlapIdxs, parentClause);
7047
7048 lowAddr = builder.CreatePointerCast(mapData.Pointers[mapDataIndex],
7049 builder.getPtrTy());
7050 highAddr = builder.CreatePointerCast(
7051 builder.CreateConstGEP1_32(mapData.BaseType[mapDataIndex],
7052 mapData.Pointers[mapDataIndex], 1),
7053 builder.getPtrTy());
7054
7055 // Currently, the return parameter should be the over-riding parent in
7056 // cases where we have a return parameter that is echoed to all members,
7057 // the main case of this currently is with fortran descriptors. It may
7058 // need more finessing for C/C++ in the future or descriptors that are
7059 // members of derived types.
7060 mapFlag &= ~llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags::OMP_MAP_RETURN_PARAM;
7061
7062 // TODO: We may want to skip arrays/array sections in this as Clang does.
7063 // It appears to be an optimisation rather than a necessity though,
7064 // but this requires further investigation. However, we would have to make
7065 // sure to not exclude maps with bounds that ARE pointers, as these are
7066 // processed as separate components, i.e. pointer + data.
7067 for (auto v : overlapIdxs) {
7068 auto mapDataOverlapIdx = getMapDataMemberIdx(
7069 mapData,
7070 cast<omp::MapInfoOp>(parentClause.getMembers()[v].getDefiningOp()));
7071 auto isPtrMap = checkIfPointerMap(
7072 llvm::cast<omp::MapInfoOp>(mapData.MapClause[mapDataOverlapIdx]));
7073 combinedInfo.Types.emplace_back(mapFlag);
7074 combinedInfo.DevicePointers.emplace_back(
7075 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::DeviceInfoTy::None);
7076 combinedInfo.Names.emplace_back(LLVM::createMappingInformation(
7077 mapData.MapClause[mapDataIndex]->getLoc(), ompBuilder));
7078 combinedInfo.BasePointers.emplace_back(
7079 mapData.BasePointers[mapDataIndex]);
7080 combinedInfo.Mappers.emplace_back(nullptr);
7081 combinedInfo.Pointers.emplace_back(lowAddr);
7082 auto sizeCalc = builder.CreateIntCast(
7083 builder.CreatePtrDiff(builder.getInt8Ty(),
7084 mapData.OriginalValue[mapDataOverlapIdx],
7085 lowAddr),
7086 builder.getInt64Ty(), /*isSigned=*/true);
7087 // In certain cases, we'll generate a size of 0 if we're not careful
7088 // (e.g. if lowAddr happens to be the first member), which isn't
7089 // correct, even if the runtimes is sometimes fine with it so, in these
7090 // scenarios we select the types size instead.
7091 auto sizeSel = builder.CreateSelect(
7092 builder.CreateICmpNE(builder.getInt64(0), sizeCalc), sizeCalc,
7093 isPtrMap ? llvm::ConstantExpr::getSizeOf(builder.getPtrTy())
7094 : mapData.Sizes[mapDataOverlapIdx]);
7095 combinedInfo.Sizes.emplace_back(sizeSel);
7096 lowAddr = builder.CreateConstGEP1_32(
7097 isPtrMap ? builder.getPtrTy() : mapData.BaseType[mapDataOverlapIdx],
7098 mapData.BasePointers[mapDataOverlapIdx], 1);
7099 }
7100
7101 combinedInfo.Types.emplace_back(mapFlag);
7102 combinedInfo.DevicePointers.emplace_back(
7103 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::DeviceInfoTy::None);
7104 combinedInfo.Names.emplace_back(LLVM::createMappingInformation(
7105 mapData.MapClause[mapDataIndex]->getLoc(), ompBuilder));
7106 combinedInfo.BasePointers.emplace_back(
7107 mapData.BasePointers[mapDataIndex]);
7108 combinedInfo.Mappers.emplace_back(nullptr);
7109 combinedInfo.Pointers.emplace_back(lowAddr);
7110 combinedInfo.Sizes.emplace_back(builder.CreateIntCast(
7111 builder.CreatePtrDiff(builder.getInt8Ty(), highAddr, lowAddr),
7112 builder.getInt64Ty(), true));
7113 }
7114 }
7115}
7116
7117static void processMapWithMembersOf(LLVM::ModuleTranslation &moduleTranslation,
7118 llvm::IRBuilderBase &builder,
7119 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder &ompBuilder,
7120 DataLayout &dl, MapInfosTy &combinedInfo,
7121 MapInfoData &mapData, uint64_t mapDataIndex,
7122 TargetDirectiveEnumTy targetDirective) {
7123 assert(!ompBuilder.Config.isTargetDevice() &&
7124 "function only supported for host device codegen");
7125
7126 auto parentClause =
7127 llvm::cast<omp::MapInfoOp>(mapData.MapClause[mapDataIndex]);
7128
7129 // If we have a partial map (no parent referenced in the map clauses of the
7130 // directive, only members) and only a single member, we do not need to bind
7131 // the map of the member to the parent, we can pass the member separately.
7132 if (parentClause.getMembers().size() == 1 && parentClause.getPartialMap()) {
7133 auto memberClause = llvm::cast<omp::MapInfoOp>(
7134 parentClause.getMembers()[0].getDefiningOp());
7135 int memberDataIdx = getMapDataMemberIdx(mapData, memberClause);
7136 // Note: Clang treats arrays with explicit bounds that fall into this
7137 // category as a parent with map case, however, it seems this isn't a
7138 // requirement, and processing them as an individual map is fine. So,
7139 // we will handle them as individual maps for the moment, as it's
7140 // difficult for us to check this as we always require bounds to be
7141 // specified currently and it's also marginally more optimal (single
7142 // map rather than two). The difference may come from the fact that
7143 // Clang maps array without bounds as pointers (which we do not
7144 // currently do), whereas we treat them as arrays in all cases
7145 // currently.
7146 processIndividualMap(
7147 builder, ompBuilder, mapData, memberDataIdx, combinedInfo,
7148 targetDirective,
7149 /*MemberOfFlag=*/llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags::OMP_MAP_NONE,
7150 /*isTargetParam=*/true, mapDataIndex);
7151 return;
7152 }
7153
7154 auto collectMapInfoIdxs =
7155 [&](llvm::SmallVectorImpl<int64_t> &mapsAndInfoIdx) {
7156 auto parentClause =
7157 llvm::cast<omp::MapInfoOp>(mapData.MapClause[mapDataIndex]);
7158 mapsAndInfoIdx.push_back(getMapDataMemberIdx(mapData, parentClause));
7159 for (auto member : parentClause.getMembers())
7160 mapsAndInfoIdx.push_back(getMapDataMemberIdx(
7161 mapData, llvm::cast<omp::MapInfoOp>(member.getDefiningOp())));
7162 };
7163
7164 llvm::SmallVector<int64_t> mapInfoIdx;
7165 collectMapInfoIdxs(mapInfoIdx);
7166
7167 llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags memberOfFlag =
7168 ompBuilder.getMemberOfFlag(combinedInfo.Types.size());
7169 for (size_t i = 0; i < mapInfoIdx.size(); i++) {
7170 // Index == 0 is the parent map and if it gets here it's an unattachable
7171 // type and should have OMP_MAP_TARGET_PARAM applied and no MEMBER_OF flag.
7172 if (i == 0) {
7173 mapParentWithMembers(moduleTranslation, builder, ompBuilder, dl,
7174 combinedInfo, mapData, mapInfoIdx[i], memberOfFlag,
7175 targetDirective);
7176 } else {
7177 processIndividualMap(builder, ompBuilder, mapData, mapInfoIdx[i],
7178 combinedInfo, targetDirective, memberOfFlag,
7179 /*isTargetParam=*/false, mapDataIndex);
7180 }
7181 }
7182}
7183
7184// This is a variation on Clang's GenerateOpenMPCapturedVars, which
7185// generates different operation (e.g. load/store) combinations for
7186// arguments to the kernel, based on map capture kinds which are then
7187// utilised in the combinedInfo in place of the original Map value.
7188static void
7189createAlteredByCaptureMap(MapInfoData &mapData,
7190 LLVM::ModuleTranslation &moduleTranslation,
7191 llvm::IRBuilderBase &builder) {
7192 assert(!moduleTranslation.getOpenMPBuilder()->Config.isTargetDevice() &&
7193 "function only supported for host device codegen");
7194 for (size_t i = 0; i < mapData.MapClause.size(); ++i) {
7195 auto mapOp = cast<omp::MapInfoOp>(mapData.MapClause[i]);
7196 bool isAttachMap =
7197 ((convertClauseMapFlags(mapOp.getMapType()) &
7198 llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags::OMP_MAP_ATTACH) ==
7199 llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags::OMP_MAP_ATTACH);
7200
7201 // If it's declare target, skip it, it's handled separately. However, if
7202 // it's declare target, and an attach map, we want to calculate the exact
7203 // address offset so that we attach correctly.
7204 if (!mapData.IsDeclareTarget[i] ||
7205 (mapData.IsDeclareTarget[i] && isAttachMap)) {
7206 omp::VariableCaptureKind captureKind = mapOp.getMapCaptureType();
7207 bool isPtrTy = checkIfPointerMap(mapOp);
7208
7209 // Currently handles array sectioning lowerbound case, but more
7210 // logic may be required in the future. Clang invokes EmitLValue,
7211 // which has specialised logic for special Clang types such as user
7212 // defines, so it is possible we will have to extend this for
7213 // structures or other complex types. As the general idea is that this
7214 // function mimics some of the logic from Clang that we require for
7215 // kernel argument passing from host -> device.
7216 switch (captureKind) {
7217 case omp::VariableCaptureKind::ByRef: {
7218 llvm::Value *newV = mapData.Pointers[i];
7219 std::vector<llvm::Value *> offsetIdx = calculateBoundsOffset(
7220 moduleTranslation, builder, mapData.BaseType[i]->isArrayTy(),
7221 mapOp.getBounds());
7222 if (isPtrTy)
7223 newV = builder.CreateLoad(builder.getPtrTy(), newV);
7224
7225 if (!offsetIdx.empty())
7226 newV = builder.CreateInBoundsGEP(mapData.BaseType[i], newV, offsetIdx,
7227 "array_offset");
7228 mapData.Pointers[i] = newV;
7229 } break;
7230 case omp::VariableCaptureKind::ByCopy: {
7231 llvm::Type *type = mapData.BaseType[i];
7232 llvm::Value *newV;
7233 if (mapData.Pointers[i]->getType()->isPointerTy())
7234 newV = builder.CreateLoad(type, mapData.Pointers[i]);
7235 else
7236 newV = mapData.Pointers[i];
7237
7238 if (!isPtrTy) {
7239 auto curInsert = builder.saveIP();
7240 llvm::DebugLoc DbgLoc = builder.getCurrentDebugLocation();
7241 builder.restoreIP(findAllocInsertPoints(builder, moduleTranslation));
7242 auto *memTempAlloc =
7243 builder.CreateAlloca(builder.getPtrTy(), nullptr, ".casted");
7244 builder.SetCurrentDebugLocation(DbgLoc);
7245 builder.restoreIP(curInsert);
7246
7247 builder.CreateStore(newV, memTempAlloc);
7248 newV = builder.CreateLoad(builder.getPtrTy(), memTempAlloc);
7249 }
7250
7251 mapData.Pointers[i] = newV;
7252 mapData.BasePointers[i] = newV;
7253 } break;
7254 case omp::VariableCaptureKind::This:
7255 case omp::VariableCaptureKind::VLAType:
7256 mapData.MapClause[i]->emitOpError("Unhandled capture kind");
7257 break;
7258 }
7259 }
7260 }
7261}
7262
7263// Generate all map related information and fill the combinedInfo.
7264static void genMapInfos(llvm::IRBuilderBase &builder,
7265 LLVM::ModuleTranslation &moduleTranslation,
7266 DataLayout &dl, MapInfosTy &combinedInfo,
7267 MapInfoData &mapData,
7268 TargetDirectiveEnumTy targetDirective) {
7269 assert(!moduleTranslation.getOpenMPBuilder()->Config.isTargetDevice() &&
7270 "function only supported for host device codegen");
7271 // We wish to modify some of the methods in which arguments are
7272 // passed based on their capture type by the target region, this can
7273 // involve generating new loads and stores, which changes the
7274 // MLIR value to LLVM value mapping, however, we only wish to do this
7275 // locally for the current function/target and also avoid altering
7276 // ModuleTranslation, so we remap the base pointer or pointer stored
7277 // in the map infos corresponding MapInfoData, which is later accessed
7278 // by genMapInfos and createTarget to help generate the kernel and
7279 // kernel arg structure. It primarily becomes relevant in cases like
7280 // bycopy, or byref range'd arrays. In the default case, we simply
7281 // pass thee pointer byref as both basePointer and pointer.
7282 createAlteredByCaptureMap(mapData, moduleTranslation, builder);
7283
7284 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder *ompBuilder = moduleTranslation.getOpenMPBuilder();
7285
7286 // We operate under the assumption that all vectors that are
7287 // required in MapInfoData are of equal lengths (either filled with
7288 // default constructed data or appropiate information) so we can
7289 // utilise the size from any component of MapInfoData, if we can't
7290 // something is missing from the initial MapInfoData construction.
7291 for (size_t i = 0; i < mapData.MapClause.size(); ++i) {
7292 if (mapData.IsAMember[i])
7293 continue;
7294
7295 auto mapInfoOp = dyn_cast<omp::MapInfoOp>(mapData.MapClause[i]);
7296 if (!mapInfoOp.getMembers().empty()) {
7297 processMapWithMembersOf(moduleTranslation, builder, *ompBuilder, dl,
7298 combinedInfo, mapData, i, targetDirective);
7299 continue;
7300 }
7301
7302 processIndividualMap(builder, *ompBuilder, mapData, i, combinedInfo,
7303 targetDirective);
7304 }
7305}
7306
7307static llvm::Expected<llvm::Function *>
7308emitUserDefinedMapper(Operation *declMapperOp, llvm::IRBuilderBase &builder,
7309 LLVM::ModuleTranslation &moduleTranslation,
7310 llvm::StringRef mapperFuncName,
7311 TargetDirectiveEnumTy targetDirective);
7312
7313static llvm::Expected<llvm::Function *>
7314getOrCreateUserDefinedMapperFunc(Operation *op, llvm::IRBuilderBase &builder,
7315 LLVM::ModuleTranslation &moduleTranslation,
7316 TargetDirectiveEnumTy targetDirective) {
7317 assert(!moduleTranslation.getOpenMPBuilder()->Config.isTargetDevice() &&
7318 "function only supported for host device codegen");
7319 auto declMapperOp = cast<omp::DeclareMapperOp>(op);
7320 std::string mapperFuncName =
7321 moduleTranslation.getOpenMPBuilder()->createPlatformSpecificName(
7322 {"omp_mapper", declMapperOp.getSymName()});
7323
7324 if (auto *lookupFunc = moduleTranslation.lookupFunction(mapperFuncName))
7325 return lookupFunc;
7326
7327 // Recursive types can cause re-entrant mapper emission. The mapper function
7328 // is created by OpenMPIRBuilder before the callbacks run, so it may already
7329 // exist in the LLVM module even though it is not yet registered in the
7330 // ModuleTranslation mapping table. Reuse and register it to break the
7331 // recursion.
7332 if (llvm::Function *existingFunc =
7333 moduleTranslation.getLLVMModule()->getFunction(mapperFuncName)) {
7334 moduleTranslation.mapFunction(mapperFuncName, existingFunc);
7335 return existingFunc;
7336 }
7337
7338 return emitUserDefinedMapper(declMapperOp, builder, moduleTranslation,
7339 mapperFuncName, targetDirective);
7340}
7341
7342static llvm::Expected<llvm::Function *>
7343emitUserDefinedMapper(Operation *op, llvm::IRBuilderBase &builder,
7344 LLVM::ModuleTranslation &moduleTranslation,
7345 llvm::StringRef mapperFuncName,
7346 TargetDirectiveEnumTy targetDirective) {
7347 assert(!moduleTranslation.getOpenMPBuilder()->Config.isTargetDevice() &&
7348 "function only supported for host device codegen");
7349 auto declMapperOp = cast<omp::DeclareMapperOp>(op);
7350 auto declMapperInfoOp = declMapperOp.getDeclareMapperInfo();
7351 if (failed(checkImplementationStatus(*declMapperInfoOp)))
7352 return llvm::make_error<PreviouslyReportedError>();
7353
7354 DataLayout dl = DataLayout(declMapperOp->getParentOfType<ModuleOp>());
7355 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder *ompBuilder = moduleTranslation.getOpenMPBuilder();
7356 llvm::Type *varType = moduleTranslation.convertType(declMapperOp.getType());
7357 SmallVector<Value> mapVars = declMapperInfoOp.getMapVars();
7358
7359 using InsertPointTy = llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::InsertPointTy;
7360
7361 // Fill up the arrays with all the mapped variables.
7362 MapInfosTy combinedInfo;
7363 auto genMapInfoCB =
7364 [&](InsertPointTy codeGenIP, llvm::Value *ptrPHI,
7365 llvm::Value *unused2) -> llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::MapInfosOrErrorTy {
7366 builder.restoreIP(codeGenIP);
7367 moduleTranslation.mapValue(declMapperOp.getSymVal(), ptrPHI);
7368 moduleTranslation.mapBlock(&declMapperOp.getRegion().front(),
7369 builder.GetInsertBlock());
7370 if (failed(moduleTranslation.convertBlock(declMapperOp.getRegion().front(),
7371 /*ignoreArguments=*/true,
7372 builder)))
7373 return llvm::make_error<PreviouslyReportedError>();
7374 MapInfoData mapData;
7375 collectMapDataFromMapOperands(mapData, mapVars, moduleTranslation, dl,
7376 builder);
7377 genMapInfos(builder, moduleTranslation, dl, combinedInfo, mapData,
7378 targetDirective);
7379
7380 // Drop the mapping that is no longer necessary so that the same region
7381 // can be processed multiple times.
7382 moduleTranslation.forgetMapping(declMapperOp.getRegion());
7383 return combinedInfo;
7384 };
7385
7386 auto customMapperCB = [&](unsigned i) -> llvm::Expected<llvm::Function *> {
7387 if (!combinedInfo.Mappers[i])
7388 return nullptr;
7389 return getOrCreateUserDefinedMapperFunc(combinedInfo.Mappers[i], builder,
7390 moduleTranslation, targetDirective);
7391 };
7392
7393 llvm::Expected<llvm::Function *> newFn = ompBuilder->emitUserDefinedMapper(
7394 genMapInfoCB, varType, mapperFuncName, customMapperCB,
7395 /*PreserveMemberOfFlags=*/true);
7396 if (!newFn)
7397 return newFn.takeError();
7398 if ([[maybe_unused]] llvm::Function *mappedFunc =
7399 moduleTranslation.lookupFunction(mapperFuncName)) {
7400 assert(mappedFunc == *newFn &&
7401 "mapper function mapping disagrees with emitted function");
7402 } else {
7403 moduleTranslation.mapFunction(mapperFuncName, *newFn);
7404 }
7405 return *newFn;
7406}
7407
7408static LogicalResult
7409convertOmpTargetData(Operation *op, llvm::IRBuilderBase &builder,
7410 LLVM::ModuleTranslation &moduleTranslation) {
7411 llvm::Value *ifCond = nullptr;
7412 llvm::Value *deviceID = builder.getInt64(llvm::omp::OMP_DEVICEID_UNDEF);
7413 SmallVector<Value> mapVars;
7414 SmallVector<Value> useDevicePtrVars;
7415 SmallVector<Value> useDeviceAddrVars;
7416 llvm::omp::RuntimeFunction RTLFn;
7417 DataLayout DL = DataLayout(op->getParentOfType<ModuleOp>());
7418 TargetDirectiveEnumTy targetDirective = getTargetDirectiveEnumTyFromOp(op);
7419
7420 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder *ompBuilder = moduleTranslation.getOpenMPBuilder();
7421 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::TargetDataInfo info(
7422 /*RequiresDevicePointerInfo=*/true,
7423 /*SeparateBeginEndCalls=*/true);
7424 assert(!ompBuilder->Config.isTargetDevice() &&
7425 "target data/enter/exit/update are host ops");
7426 bool isOffloadEntry = !ompBuilder->Config.TargetTriples.empty();
7427
7428 auto getDeviceID = [&](mlir::Value dev) -> llvm::Value * {
7429 llvm::Value *v = moduleTranslation.lookupValue(dev);
7430 return builder.CreateIntCast(v, builder.getInt64Ty(), /*isSigned=*/true);
7431 };
7432
7433 LogicalResult result =
7434 llvm::TypeSwitch<Operation *, LogicalResult>(op)
7435 .Case([&](omp::TargetDataOp dataOp) {
7436 if (failed(checkImplementationStatus(*dataOp)))
7437 return failure();
7438
7439 if (auto ifVar = dataOp.getIfExpr())
7440 ifCond = moduleTranslation.lookupValue(ifVar);
7441
7442 if (mlir::Value devId = dataOp.getDevice())
7443 deviceID = getDeviceID(devId);
7444
7445 mapVars = dataOp.getMapVars();
7446 useDevicePtrVars = dataOp.getUseDevicePtrVars();
7447 useDeviceAddrVars = dataOp.getUseDeviceAddrVars();
7448 return success();
7449 })
7450 .Case([&](omp::TargetEnterDataOp enterDataOp) -> LogicalResult {
7451 if (failed(checkImplementationStatus(*enterDataOp)))
7452 return failure();
7453
7454 if (auto ifVar = enterDataOp.getIfExpr())
7455 ifCond = moduleTranslation.lookupValue(ifVar);
7456
7457 if (mlir::Value devId = enterDataOp.getDevice())
7458 deviceID = getDeviceID(devId);
7459
7460 RTLFn =
7461 enterDataOp.getNowait()
7462 ? llvm::omp::OMPRTL___tgt_target_data_begin_nowait_mapper
7463 : llvm::omp::OMPRTL___tgt_target_data_begin_mapper;
7464 mapVars = enterDataOp.getMapVars();
7465 info.HasNoWait = enterDataOp.getNowait();
7466 return success();
7467 })
