7468 .Case([&](omp::TargetExitDataOp exitDataOp) -> LogicalResult {
7469 if (failed(checkImplementationStatus(*exitDataOp)))
7470 return failure();
7471
7472 if (auto ifVar = exitDataOp.getIfExpr())
7473 ifCond = moduleTranslation.lookupValue(ifVar);
7474
7475 if (mlir::Value devId = exitDataOp.getDevice())
7476 deviceID = getDeviceID(devId);
7477
7478 RTLFn = exitDataOp.getNowait()
7479 ? llvm::omp::OMPRTL___tgt_target_data_end_nowait_mapper
7480 : llvm::omp::OMPRTL___tgt_target_data_end_mapper;
7481 mapVars = exitDataOp.getMapVars();
7482 info.HasNoWait = exitDataOp.getNowait();
7483 return success();
7484 })
7485 .Case([&](omp::TargetUpdateOp updateDataOp) -> LogicalResult {
7486 if (failed(checkImplementationStatus(*updateDataOp)))
7487 return failure();
7488
7489 if (auto ifVar = updateDataOp.getIfExpr())
7490 ifCond = moduleTranslation.lookupValue(ifVar);
7491
7492 if (mlir::Value devId = updateDataOp.getDevice())
7493 deviceID = getDeviceID(devId);
7494
7495 RTLFn =
7496 updateDataOp.getNowait()
7497 ? llvm::omp::OMPRTL___tgt_target_data_update_nowait_mapper
7498 : llvm::omp::OMPRTL___tgt_target_data_update_mapper;
7499 mapVars = updateDataOp.getMapVars();
7500 info.HasNoWait = updateDataOp.getNowait();
7501 return success();
7502 })
7503 .DefaultUnreachable("unexpected operation");
7504
7505 if (failed(result))
7506 return failure();
7507 // Pretend we have IF(false) if we're not doing offload.
7508 if (!isOffloadEntry)
7509 ifCond = builder.getFalse();
7510
7511 using InsertPointTy = llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::InsertPointTy;
7512 MapInfoData mapData;
7513 collectMapDataFromMapOperands(mapData, mapVars, moduleTranslation, DL,
7514 builder, useDevicePtrVars, useDeviceAddrVars);
7515
7516 // Fill up the arrays with all the mapped variables.
7517 MapInfosTy combinedInfo;
7518 auto genMapInfoCB = [&](InsertPointTy codeGenIP) -> MapInfosTy & {
7519 builder.restoreIP(codeGenIP);
7520 genMapInfos(builder, moduleTranslation, DL, combinedInfo, mapData,
7521 targetDirective);
7522 return combinedInfo;
7523 };
7524
7525 // Define a lambda to apply mappings between use_device_addr and
7526 // use_device_ptr base pointers, and their associated block arguments.
7527 auto mapUseDevice =
7528 [&moduleTranslation](
7529 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::DeviceInfoTy type,
7530 llvm::ArrayRef<BlockArgument> blockArgs,
7531 llvm::SmallVectorImpl<Value> &useDeviceVars, MapInfoData &mapInfoData,
7532 llvm::function_ref<llvm::Value *(llvm::Value *)> mapper = nullptr) {
7533 for (auto [arg, useDevVar] :
7534 llvm::zip_equal(blockArgs, useDeviceVars)) {
7535
7536 auto getMapBasePtr = [](omp::MapInfoOp mapInfoOp) {
7537 return mapInfoOp.getVarPtrPtr() ? mapInfoOp.getVarPtrPtr()
7538 : mapInfoOp.getVarPtr();
7539 };
7540
7541 auto useDevMap = cast<omp::MapInfoOp>(useDevVar.getDefiningOp());
7542 for (auto [mapClause, devicePointer, basePointer] : llvm::zip_equal(
7543 mapInfoData.MapClause, mapInfoData.DevicePointers,
7544 mapInfoData.BasePointers)) {
7545 auto mapOp = cast<omp::MapInfoOp>(mapClause);
7546 if (getMapBasePtr(mapOp) != getMapBasePtr(useDevMap) ||
7547 devicePointer != type)
7548 continue;
7549
7550 if (llvm::Value *devPtrInfoMap =
7551 mapper ? mapper(basePointer) : basePointer) {
7552 moduleTranslation.mapValue(arg, devPtrInfoMap);
7553 break;
7554 }
7555 }
7556 }
7557 };
7558
7559 using BodyGenTy = llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::BodyGenTy;
7560 auto bodyGenCB = [&](InsertPointTy codeGenIP, BodyGenTy bodyGenType)
7561 -> llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::InsertPointOrErrorTy {
7562 // We must always restoreIP regardless of doing anything the caller
7563 // does not restore it, leading to incorrect (no) branch generation.
7564 builder.restoreIP(codeGenIP);
7565 assert(isa<omp::TargetDataOp>(op) &&
7566 "BodyGen requested for non TargetDataOp");
7567 auto blockArgIface = cast<omp::BlockArgOpenMPOpInterface>(op);
7568 Region &region = cast<omp::TargetDataOp>(op).getRegion();
7569 switch (bodyGenType) {
7570 case BodyGenTy::Priv:
7571 // Check if any device ptr/addr info is available
7572 if (!info.DevicePtrInfoMap.empty()) {
7573 mapUseDevice(llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::DeviceInfoTy::Address,
7574 blockArgIface.getUseDeviceAddrBlockArgs(),
7575 useDeviceAddrVars, mapData,
7576 [&](llvm::Value *basePointer) -> llvm::Value * {
7577 if (!info.DevicePtrInfoMap[basePointer].second)
7578 return nullptr;
7579 return builder.CreateLoad(
7580 builder.getPtrTy(),
7581 info.DevicePtrInfoMap[basePointer].second);
7582 });
7583 mapUseDevice(llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::DeviceInfoTy::Pointer,
7584 blockArgIface.getUseDevicePtrBlockArgs(), useDevicePtrVars,
7585 mapData, [&](llvm::Value *basePointer) {
7586 return info.DevicePtrInfoMap[basePointer].second;
7587 });
7588
7589 if (failed(inlineConvertOmpRegions(region, "omp.data.region", builder,
7590 moduleTranslation)))
7591 return llvm::make_error<PreviouslyReportedError>();
7592 }
7593 break;
7594 case BodyGenTy::DupNoPriv:
7595 if (info.DevicePtrInfoMap.empty()) {
7596 // For host device we still need to do the mapping for codegen,
7597 // otherwise it may try to lookup a missing value.
7598 mapUseDevice(llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::DeviceInfoTy::Address,
7599 blockArgIface.getUseDeviceAddrBlockArgs(),
7600 useDeviceAddrVars, mapData);
7601 mapUseDevice(llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::DeviceInfoTy::Pointer,
7602 blockArgIface.getUseDevicePtrBlockArgs(), useDevicePtrVars,
7603 mapData);
7604 }
7605 break;
7606 case BodyGenTy::NoPriv:
7607 // If device info is available then region has already been generated
7608 if (info.DevicePtrInfoMap.empty()) {
7609 if (failed(inlineConvertOmpRegions(region, "omp.data.region", builder,
7610 moduleTranslation)))
7611 return llvm::make_error<PreviouslyReportedError>();
7612 }
7613 break;
7614 }
7615 return builder.saveIP();
7616 };
7617
7618 auto customMapperCB =
7619 [&](unsigned int i) -> llvm::Expected<llvm::Function *> {
7620 if (!combinedInfo.Mappers[i])
7621 return nullptr;
7622 info.HasMapper = true;
7623 return getOrCreateUserDefinedMapperFunc(combinedInfo.Mappers[i], builder,
7624 moduleTranslation, targetDirective);
7625 };
7626
7627 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::LocationDescription ompLoc(builder);
7628 llvm::SmallVector<llvm::BasicBlock *> deallocBlocks;
7629 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::InsertPointTy allocaIP =
7630 findAllocInsertPoints(builder, moduleTranslation, &deallocBlocks);
7631 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::InsertPointOrErrorTy afterIP = [&]() {
7632 if (isa<omp::TargetDataOp>(op))
7633 return ompBuilder->createTargetData(ompLoc, allocaIP, builder.saveIP(),
7634 deallocBlocks, deviceID, ifCond, info,
7635 genMapInfoCB, customMapperCB,
7636 /*MapperFunc=*/nullptr, bodyGenCB,
7637 /*DeviceAddrCB=*/nullptr);
7638 return ompBuilder->createTargetData(ompLoc, allocaIP, builder.saveIP(),
7639 deallocBlocks, deviceID, ifCond, info,
7640 genMapInfoCB, customMapperCB, &RTLFn);
7641 }();
7642
7643 if (failed(handleError(afterIP, *op)))
7644 return failure();
7645
7646 builder.restoreIP(*afterIP);
7647 return success();
7648}
7649
7650static LogicalResult
7651convertOmpDistribute(Operation &opInst, llvm::IRBuilderBase &builder,
7652 LLVM::ModuleTranslation &moduleTranslation) {
7653 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder *ompBuilder = moduleTranslation.getOpenMPBuilder();
7654 auto distributeOp = cast<omp::DistributeOp>(opInst);
7655 if (failed(checkImplementationStatus(opInst)))
7656 return failure();
7657
7658 /// Process teams op reduction in distribute if the reduction is contained in
7659 /// this specific distribute op.
7660 omp::TeamsOp teamsOp = opInst.getParentOfType<omp::TeamsOp>();
7661 bool doDistributeReduction =
7662 teamsOp && getDistributeCapturingTeamsReduction(teamsOp) == distributeOp;
7663
7664 DenseMap<Value, llvm::Value *> reductionVariableMap;
7665 unsigned numReductionVars = teamsOp ? teamsOp.getNumReductionVars() : 0;
7666 SmallVector<omp::DeclareReductionOp> reductionDecls;
7667 SmallVector<llvm::Value *> privateReductionVariables(numReductionVars);
7668 llvm::ArrayRef<bool> isByRef;
7669
7670 if (doDistributeReduction) {
7671 isByRef = getIsByRef(teamsOp.getReductionByref());
7672 assert(isByRef.size() == teamsOp.getNumReductionVars());
7673
7674 collectReductionDecls(teamsOp, reductionDecls);
7675 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::InsertPointTy allocaIP =
7676 findAllocInsertPoints(builder, moduleTranslation);
7677
7678 MutableArrayRef<BlockArgument> reductionArgs =
7679 llvm::cast<omp::BlockArgOpenMPOpInterface>(*teamsOp)
7680 .getReductionBlockArgs();
7681
7682 if (failed(allocAndInitializeReductionVars(
7683 teamsOp, reductionArgs, builder, moduleTranslation, allocaIP,
7684 reductionDecls, privateReductionVariables, reductionVariableMap,
7685 isByRef)))
7686 return failure();
7687 }
7688
7689 using InsertPointTy = llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::InsertPointTy;
7690 auto bodyGenCB =
7691 [&](InsertPointTy allocaIP, InsertPointTy codeGenIP,
7692 llvm::ArrayRef<llvm::BasicBlock *> deallocBlocks) -> llvm::Error {
7693 // Save the alloca insertion point on ModuleTranslation stack for use in
7694 // nested regions.
7695 LLVM::ModuleTranslation::SaveStack<OpenMPAllocStackFrame> frame(
7696 moduleTranslation, allocaIP, deallocBlocks);
7697
7698 // DistributeOp has only one region associated with it.
7699 builder.restoreIP(codeGenIP);
7700 PrivateVarsInfo privVarsInfo(distributeOp);
7701
7702 llvm::Expected<llvm::BasicBlock *> afterAllocas = allocatePrivateVars(
7703 distributeOp, builder, moduleTranslation, privVarsInfo, allocaIP);
7704 if (handleError(afterAllocas, opInst).failed())
7705 return llvm::make_error<PreviouslyReportedError>();
7706
7707 if (handleError(initPrivateVars(builder, moduleTranslation, privVarsInfo),
7708 opInst)
7709 .failed())
7710 return llvm::make_error<PreviouslyReportedError>();
7711
7712 if (failed(copyFirstPrivateVars(
7713 distributeOp, builder, moduleTranslation, privVarsInfo.mlirVars,
7714 privVarsInfo.llvmVars, privVarsInfo.privatizers,
7715 distributeOp.getPrivateNeedsBarrier())))
7716 return llvm::make_error<PreviouslyReportedError>();
7717
7718 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder *ompBuilder = moduleTranslation.getOpenMPBuilder();
7719 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::LocationDescription ompLoc(builder);
7720 llvm::Expected<llvm::BasicBlock *> regionBlock =
7721 convertOmpOpRegions(distributeOp.getRegion(), "omp.distribute.region",
7722 builder, moduleTranslation);
7723 if (!regionBlock)
7724 return regionBlock.takeError();
7725 builder.SetInsertPoint(*regionBlock, (*regionBlock)->begin());
7726
7727 // Skip applying a workshare loop below when translating 'distribute
7728 // parallel do' (it's been already handled by this point while translating
7729 // the nested omp.wsloop).
7730 if (!isa_and_present<omp::WsloopOp>(distributeOp.getNestedWrapper())) {
7731 // TODO: Add support for clauses which are valid for DISTRIBUTE
7732 // constructs. Static schedule is the default.
7733 bool hasDistSchedule = distributeOp.getDistScheduleStatic();
7734 auto schedule = hasDistSchedule ? omp::ClauseScheduleKind::Distribute
7735 : omp::ClauseScheduleKind::Static;
7736 // dist_schedule clauses are ordered - otherise this should be false
7737 bool isOrdered = hasDistSchedule;
7738 std::optional<omp::ScheduleModifier> scheduleMod;
7739 bool isSimd = false;
7740 llvm::omp::WorksharingLoopType workshareLoopType =
7741 llvm::omp::WorksharingLoopType::DistributeStaticLoop;
7742 bool loopNeedsBarrier = false;
7743 llvm::Value *chunk = moduleTranslation.lookupValue(
7744 distributeOp.getDistScheduleChunkSize());
7745 llvm::CanonicalLoopInfo *loopInfo =
7746 findCurrentLoopInfo(moduleTranslation);
7747 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::InsertPointOrErrorTy wsloopIP =
7748 ompBuilder->applyWorkshareLoop(
7749 ompLoc.DL, loopInfo, allocaIP, loopNeedsBarrier,
7750 convertToScheduleKind(schedule), chunk, isSimd,
7751 scheduleMod == omp::ScheduleModifier::monotonic,
7752 scheduleMod == omp::ScheduleModifier::nonmonotonic, isOrdered,
7753 workshareLoopType, false, hasDistSchedule, chunk);
7754
7755 if (!wsloopIP)
7756 return wsloopIP.takeError();
7757 }
7758 if (failed(cleanupPrivateVars(distributeOp, builder, moduleTranslation,
7759 distributeOp.getLoc(), privVarsInfo)))
7760 return llvm::make_error<PreviouslyReportedError>();
7761
7762 return llvm::Error::success();
7763 };
7764
7765 llvm::SmallVector<llvm::BasicBlock *> deallocBlocks;
7766 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::InsertPointTy allocaIP =
7767 findAllocInsertPoints(builder, moduleTranslation, &deallocBlocks);
7768 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::LocationDescription ompLoc(builder);
7769 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::InsertPointOrErrorTy afterIP =
7770 ompBuilder->createDistribute(ompLoc, allocaIP, deallocBlocks, bodyGenCB);
7771
7772 if (failed(handleError(afterIP, opInst)))
7773 return failure();
7774
7775 builder.restoreIP(*afterIP);
7776
7777 if (doDistributeReduction) {
7778 // Process the reductions if required.
7779 return createReductionsAndCleanup(
7780 teamsOp, builder, moduleTranslation, allocaIP, reductionDecls,
7781 privateReductionVariables, isByRef,
7782 /*isNoWait*/ false, /*isTeamsReduction*/ true);
7783 }
7784 return success();
7785}
7786
7787/// Lowers the FlagsAttr which is applied to the module when offloading. This
7788/// attribute contains OpenMP RTL globals that can be passed as flags to the
7789/// frontend, otherwise they are set to default
7790static LogicalResult
7791convertFlagsAttr(Operation *op, mlir::omp::FlagsAttr attribute,
7792 LLVM::ModuleTranslation &moduleTranslation) {
7793 auto offloadMod = dyn_cast<omp::OffloadModuleInterface>(op);
7794 if (!offloadMod)
7795 return op->emitOpError() << "omp flags attached to non offload module op";
7796
7797 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder *ompBuilder = moduleTranslation.getOpenMPBuilder();
7798
7799 ompBuilder->M.addModuleFlag(llvm::Module::Max, "openmp-device",
7800 attribute.getOpenmpDeviceVersion());
7801
7802 // The flags below are only intended to be emitted for GPU offload targets.
7803 if (!offloadMod.getIsGPU())
7804 return success();
7805
7806 if (attribute.getNoGpuLib())
7807 return success();
7808
7809 ompBuilder->createGlobalFlag(
7810 attribute.getDebugKind() /*LangOpts().OpenMPTargetDebug*/,
7811 "__omp_rtl_debug_kind");
7812 ompBuilder->createGlobalFlag(
7813 attribute
7814 .getAssumeTeamsOversubscription() /*LangOpts().OpenMPTeamSubscription*/
7815 ,
7816 "__omp_rtl_assume_teams_oversubscription");
7817 ompBuilder->createGlobalFlag(
7818 attribute
7819 .getAssumeThreadsOversubscription() /*LangOpts().OpenMPThreadSubscription*/
7820 ,
7821 "__omp_rtl_assume_threads_oversubscription");
7822 ompBuilder->createGlobalFlag(
7823 attribute.getAssumeNoThreadState() /*LangOpts().OpenMPNoThreadState*/,
7824 "__omp_rtl_assume_no_thread_state");
7825 ompBuilder->createGlobalFlag(
7826 attribute
7827 .getAssumeNoNestedParallelism() /*LangOpts().OpenMPNoNestedParallelism*/
7828 ,
7829 "__omp_rtl_assume_no_nested_parallelism");
7830 return success();
7831}
7832
7833static void getTargetEntryUniqueInfo(llvm::TargetRegionEntryInfo &targetInfo,
7834 omp::TargetOp targetOp,
7835 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder &ompBuilder,
7836 llvm::vfs::FileSystem &vfs,
7837 llvm::StringRef parentName = "") {
7838 auto fileLoc = targetOp.getLoc()->findInstanceOf<FileLineColLoc>();
7839 assert(fileLoc && "No file found from location");
7840
7841 auto fileInfoCallBack = [&fileLoc]() {
7842 return std::pair<std::string, uint64_t>(
7843 llvm::StringRef(fileLoc.getFilename()), fileLoc.getLine());
7844 };
7845
7846 targetInfo =
7847 ompBuilder.getTargetEntryUniqueInfo(fileInfoCallBack, vfs, parentName);
7848}
7849
7850static void
7851handleDeclareTargetMapVar(MapInfoData &mapData,
7852 LLVM::ModuleTranslation &moduleTranslation,
7853 llvm::IRBuilderBase &builder, llvm::Function *func) {
7854 assert(moduleTranslation.getOpenMPBuilder()->Config.isTargetDevice() &&
7855 "function only supported for target device codegen");
7856 llvm::IRBuilderBase::InsertPointGuard guard(builder);
7857 for (size_t i = 0; i < mapData.MapClause.size(); ++i) {
7858 // In the case of declare target mapped variables, the basePointer is
7859 // the reference pointer generated by the convertDeclareTargetAttr
7860 // method. Whereas the kernelValue is the original variable, so for
7861 // the device we must replace all uses of this original global variable
7862 // (stored in kernelValue) with the reference pointer (stored in
7863 // basePointer for declare target mapped variables), as for device the
7864 // data is mapped into this reference pointer and should be loaded
7865 // from it, the original variable is discarded. On host both exist and
7866 // metadata is generated (elsewhere in the convertDeclareTargetAttr)
7867 // function to link the two variables in the runtime and then both the
7868 // reference pointer and the pointer are assigned in the kernel argument
7869 // structure for the host.
7870 if (!mapData.IsDeclareTarget[i])
7871 continue;
7872 // If the original map value is a constant, then we have to make sure all
7873 // of it's uses within the current kernel/function that we are going to
7874 // rewrite are converted to instructions, as we will be altering the old
7875 // use (OriginalValue) from a constant to an instruction, which will be
7876 // illegal and ICE the compiler if the user is a constant expression of
7877 // some kind e.g. a constant GEP.
7878 if (auto *constant = dyn_cast<llvm::Constant>(mapData.OriginalValue[i]))
7879 convertUsersOfConstantsToInstructions(constant, func, false);
7880
7881 // The users iterator will get invalidated if we modify an element,
7882 // so we populate this vector of uses to alter each user on an
7883 // individual basis to emit its own load (rather than one load for
7884 // all).
7885 llvm::SmallVector<llvm::User *> userVec;
7886 for (llvm::User *user : mapData.OriginalValue[i]->users())
7887 userVec.push_back(user);
7888
7889 for (llvm::User *user : userVec) {
7890 auto *insn = dyn_cast<llvm::Instruction>(user);
7891 if (!insn || insn->getFunction() != func)
7892 continue;
7893 auto mapOp = cast<omp::MapInfoOp>(mapData.MapClause[i]);
7894 llvm::Value *substitute = mapData.BasePointers[i];
7895 auto declTarPtr =
7896 mapOp.getVarPtrPtr() ? mapOp.getVarPtrPtr() : mapOp.getVarPtr();
7897 if (isDeclareTargetLink(declTarPtr) ||
7898 (isDeclareTargetTo(declTarPtr) &&
7899 moduleTranslation.getOpenMPBuilder()
7900 ->Config.hasRequiresUnifiedSharedMemory())) {
7901 builder.SetCurrentDebugLocation(insn->getDebugLoc());
7902 substitute = builder.CreateLoad(mapData.BasePointers[i]->getType(),
7903 mapData.BasePointers[i]);
7904 cast<llvm::LoadInst>(substitute)->moveBefore(insn->getIterator());
7905 }
7906 user->replaceUsesOfWith(mapData.OriginalValue[i], substitute);
7907 }
7908 }
7909}
7910
7911// The createDeviceArgumentAccessor function generates
7912// instructions for retrieving (acessing) kernel
7913// arguments inside of the device kernel for use by
7914// the kernel. This enables different semantics such as
7915// the creation of temporary copies of data allowing
7916// semantics like read-only/no host write back kernel
7917// arguments.
7918//
7919// This currently implements a very light version of Clang's
7920// EmitParmDecl's handling of direct argument handling as well
7921// as a portion of the argument access generation based on
7922// capture types found at the end of emitOutlinedFunctionPrologue
7923// in Clang. The indirect path handling of EmitParmDecl's may be
7924// required for future work, but a direct 1-to-1 copy doesn't seem
7925// possible as the logic is rather scattered throughout Clang's
7926// lowering and perhaps we wish to deviate slightly.
7927//
7928// \param mapData - A container containing vectors of information
7929// corresponding to the input argument, which should have a
7930// corresponding entry in the MapInfoData containers
7931// OrigialValue's.
7932// \param arg - This is the generated kernel function argument that
7933// corresponds to the passed in input argument. We generated different
7934// accesses of this Argument, based on capture type and other Input
7935// related information.
7936// \param input - This is the host side value that will be passed to
7937// the kernel i.e. the kernel input, we rewrite all uses of this within
7938// the kernel (as we generate the kernel body based on the target's region
7939// which maintians references to the original input) to the retVal argument
7940// apon exit of this function inside of the OMPIRBuilder. This interlinks
7941// the kernel argument to future uses of it in the function providing
7942// appropriate "glue" instructions inbetween.
7943// \param retVal - This is the value that all uses of input inside of the
7944// kernel will be re-written to, the goal of this function is to generate
7945// an appropriate location for the kernel argument to be accessed from,
7946// e.g. ByRef will result in a temporary allocation location and then
7947// a store of the kernel argument into this allocated memory which
7948// will then be loaded from, ByCopy will use the allocated memory
7949// directly.
7950static llvm::IRBuilderBase::InsertPoint createDeviceArgumentAccessor(
7951 omp::TargetOp targetOp, MapInfoData &mapData, llvm::Argument &arg,
7952 llvm::Value *input, llvm::Value *&retVal, llvm::IRBuilderBase &builder,
7953 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder &ompBuilder,
7954 LLVM::ModuleTranslation &moduleTranslation,
7955 llvm::IRBuilderBase::InsertPoint allocaIP,
7956 llvm::IRBuilderBase::InsertPoint codeGenIP,
7957 llvm::ArrayRef<llvm::IRBuilderBase::InsertPoint> deallocIPs) {
7958 assert(ompBuilder.Config.isTargetDevice() &&
7959 "function only supported for target device codegen");
7960 builder.restoreIP(allocaIP);
7961
7962 omp::VariableCaptureKind capture = omp::VariableCaptureKind::ByRef;
7963 LLVM::TypeToLLVMIRTranslator typeToLLVMIRTranslator(
7964 ompBuilder.M.getContext());
7965 unsigned alignmentValue = 0;
7966 BlockArgument mlirArg;
7967 SmallVector<std::pair<Value, BlockArgument>> blockArgsPairs;
7968 cast<omp::BlockArgOpenMPOpInterface>(*targetOp).getBlockArgsPairs(
7969 blockArgsPairs);
7970 // Find the associated MapInfoData entry for the current input
7971 for (size_t i = 0; i < mapData.MapClause.size(); ++i) {
7972 if (mapData.OriginalValue[i] == input) {
7973 auto mapOp = cast<omp::MapInfoOp>(mapData.MapClause[i]);
7974 capture = mapOp.getMapCaptureType();
7975 // Get information of alignment of mapped object
7976 alignmentValue = typeToLLVMIRTranslator.getPreferredAlignment(
7977 mapOp.getVarPtrType(), ompBuilder.M.getDataLayout());
7978
7979 // Find the corresponding entry block argument, which can be associated to
7980 // a map, use_device* or has_device* clause.
7981 for (auto &[val, arg] : blockArgsPairs) {
7982 if (mapOp.getResult() == val) {
7983 mlirArg = arg;
7984 break;
7985 }
7986 }
7987 assert(mlirArg && "expected to find entry block argument for map clause");
7988 break;
7989 }
7990 }
7991
7992 unsigned int allocaAS = ompBuilder.M.getDataLayout().getAllocaAddrSpace();
7993 unsigned int defaultAS =
7994 ompBuilder.M.getDataLayout().getProgramAddressSpace();
7995
7996 // Create the allocation for the argument.
7997 llvm::Value *v = nullptr;
7998 if (omp::opInSharedDeviceContext(*targetOp) &&
7999 omp::allocaUsesRequireSharedMem(mlirArg)) {
8000 // Use the beginning of the codeGenIP rather than the usual allocation point
8001 // for shared memory allocations because otherwise these would be done prior
8002 // to the target initialization call. Also, the exit block (where the
8003 // deallocation is placed) is only executed if the initialization call
8004 // succeeds.
8005 builder.SetInsertPoint(codeGenIP.getBlock()->getFirstInsertionPt());
8006 v = ompBuilder.createOMPAllocShared(builder, arg.getType());
8007
8008 // Create deallocations in all provided deallocation points and then restore
8009 // the insertion point to right after the new allocations.
8010 llvm::IRBuilderBase::InsertPointGuard guard(builder);
8011 for (auto deallocIP : deallocIPs) {
8012 builder.SetInsertPoint(deallocIP.getBlock(), deallocIP.getPoint());
8013 ompBuilder.createOMPFreeShared(builder, v, arg.getType());
8014 }
8015 } else {
8016 // Use the current point, which was previously set to allocaIP.
8017 v = builder.CreateAlloca(arg.getType(), allocaAS);
8018
8019 if (allocaAS != defaultAS && arg.getType()->isPointerTy())
8020 v = builder.CreateAddrSpaceCast(v, builder.getPtrTy(defaultAS));
8021 }
8022
8023 builder.CreateStore(&arg, v);
8024
8025 builder.restoreIP(codeGenIP);
8026
8027 switch (capture) {
8028 case omp::VariableCaptureKind::ByCopy: {
8029 retVal = v;
8030 break;
8031 }
8032 case omp::VariableCaptureKind::ByRef: {
8033 llvm::LoadInst *loadInst = builder.CreateAlignedLoad(
8034 v->getType(), v,
8035 ompBuilder.M.getDataLayout().getPrefTypeAlign(v->getType()));
8036 // CreateAlignedLoad function creates similar LLVM IR:
8037 // %res = load ptr, ptr %input, align 8
8038 // This LLVM IR does not contain information about alignment
8039 // of the loaded value. We need to add !align metadata to unblock
8040 // optimizer. The existence of the !align metadata on the instruction
8041 // tells the optimizer that the value loaded is known to be aligned to
8042 // a boundary specified by the integer value in the metadata node.
8043 // Example:
8044 // %res = load ptr, ptr %input, align 8, !align !align_md_node
8045 // ^ ^
8046 // | |
8047 // alignment of %input address |
8048 // |
8049 // alignment of %res object
8050 if (v->getType()->isPointerTy() && alignmentValue) {
8051 llvm::MDBuilder MDB(builder.getContext());
8052 loadInst->setMetadata(
8053 llvm::LLVMContext::MD_align,
8054 llvm::MDNode::get(builder.getContext(),
8055 MDB.createConstant(llvm::ConstantInt::get(
8056 llvm::Type::getInt64Ty(builder.getContext()),
8057 alignmentValue))));
8058 }
8059 retVal = loadInst;
8060
8061 break;
8062 }
8063 case omp::VariableCaptureKind::This:
8064 case omp::VariableCaptureKind::VLAType:
8065 // TODO: Consider returning error to use standard reporting for
8066 // unimplemented features.
8067 assert(false && "Currently unsupported capture kind");
8068 break;
8069 }
8070
8071 return builder.saveIP();
8072}
8073
8074/// Follow uses of `host_eval`-defined block arguments of the given `omp.target`
8075/// operation and populate output variables with their corresponding host value
8076/// (i.e. operand evaluated outside of the target region), based on their uses
8077/// inside of the target region.
8078///
8079/// Loop bounds and steps are only optionally populated, if output vectors are
8080/// provided.
8081static void
8082extractHostEvalClauses(omp::TargetOp targetOp, Value &numThreads,
8083 Value &numTeamsLower, Value &numTeamsUpper,
8084 Value &threadLimit,
8085 llvm::SmallVectorImpl<Value> *lowerBounds = nullptr,
8086 llvm::SmallVectorImpl<Value> *upperBounds = nullptr,
8087 llvm::SmallVectorImpl<Value> *steps = nullptr) {
8088 auto blockArgIface = llvm::cast<omp::BlockArgOpenMPOpInterface>(*targetOp);
8089 for (auto item : llvm::zip_equal(targetOp.getHostEvalVars(),
8090 blockArgIface.getHostEvalBlockArgs())) {
8091 Value hostEvalVar = std::get<0>(item), blockArg = std::get<1>(item);
8092
8093 for (Operation *user : blockArg.getUsers()) {
8094 llvm::TypeSwitch<Operation *>(user)
8095 .Case([&](omp::TeamsOp teamsOp) {
8096 if (teamsOp.getNumTeamsLower() == blockArg)
8097 numTeamsLower = hostEvalVar;
8098 else if (llvm::is_contained(teamsOp.getNumTeamsUpperVars(),
8099 blockArg))
8100 numTeamsUpper = hostEvalVar;
8101 else if (!teamsOp.getThreadLimitVars().empty() &&
8102 teamsOp.getThreadLimit(0) == blockArg)
8103 threadLimit = hostEvalVar;
8104 else
8105 llvm_unreachable("unsupported host_eval use");
8106 })
8107 .Case([&](omp::ParallelOp parallelOp) {
8108 if (!parallelOp.getNumThreadsVars().empty() &&
8109 parallelOp.getNumThreads(0) == blockArg)
8110 numThreads = hostEvalVar;
8111 else
8112 llvm_unreachable("unsupported host_eval use");
8113 })
8114 .Case([&](omp::LoopNestOp loopOp) {
8115 auto processBounds =
8116 [&](OperandRange opBounds,
8117 llvm::SmallVectorImpl<Value> *outBounds) -> bool {
8118 bool found = false;
8119 for (auto [i, lb] : llvm::enumerate(opBounds)) {
8120 if (lb == blockArg) {
8121 found = true;
8122 if (outBounds)
8123 (*outBounds)[i] = hostEvalVar;
8124 }
8125 }
8126 return found;
8127 };
8128 bool found =
8129 processBounds(loopOp.getLoopLowerBounds(), lowerBounds);
8130 found = processBounds(loopOp.getLoopUpperBounds(), upperBounds) ||
8131 found;
8132 found = processBounds(loopOp.getLoopSteps(), steps) || found;
8133 (void)found;
8134 assert(found && "unsupported host_eval use");
8135 })
8136 .DefaultUnreachable("unsupported host_eval use");
8137 }
8138 }
8139}
8140
8141/// If \p op is of the given type parameter, return it casted to that type.
8142/// Otherwise, if its immediate parent operation (or some other higher-level
8143/// parent, if \p immediateParent is false) is of that type, return that parent
8144/// casted to the given type.
8145///
8146/// If \p op is \c null or neither it or its parent(s) are of the specified
8147/// type, return a \c null operation.
8148template <typename OpTy>
8149static OpTy castOrGetParentOfType(Operation *op, bool immediateParent = false) {
8150 if (!op)
8151 return OpTy();
8152
8153 if (OpTy casted = dyn_cast<OpTy>(op))
8154 return casted;
8155
8156 if (immediateParent)
8157 return dyn_cast_if_present<OpTy>(op->getParentOp());
8158
8159 return op->getParentOfType<OpTy>();
8160}
8161
8162/// If the given \p value is defined by an \c llvm.mlir.constant operation and
8163/// it is of an integer type, return its value.
8164static std::optional<int64_t> extractConstInteger(Value value) {
8165 if (!value)
8166 return std::nullopt;
8167
8168 if (auto constOp = value.getDefiningOp<LLVM::ConstantOp>())
8169 if (auto constAttr = dyn_cast<IntegerAttr>(constOp.getValue()))
8170 return constAttr.getInt();
8171
8172 return std::nullopt;
8173}
8174
8175static uint64_t getTypeByteSize(mlir::Type type, const DataLayout &dl) {
8176 uint64_t sizeInBits = dl.getTypeSizeInBits(type);
8177 uint64_t sizeInBytes = sizeInBits / 8;
8178 return sizeInBytes;
8179}
8180
8181template <typename OpTy>
8182static uint64_t getReductionDataSize(OpTy &op) {
8183 if (op.getNumReductionVars() > 0) {
8184 SmallVector<omp::DeclareReductionOp> reductions;
8185 collectReductionDecls(op, reductions);
8186
8187 llvm::SmallVector<mlir::Type> members;
8188 members.reserve(reductions.size());
8189 for (omp::DeclareReductionOp &red : reductions) {
8190 // For by-ref reductions, use the actual element type rather than the
8191 // pointer type so that the buffer size matches the access pattern in
8192 // the copy/reduce callbacks generated by OMPIRBuilder.
8193 if (red.getByrefElementType())
8194 members.push_back(*red.getByrefElementType());
8195 else
8196 members.push_back(red.getType());
8197 }
8198 Operation *opp = op.getOperation();
8199 auto structType = mlir::LLVM::LLVMStructType::getLiteral(
8200 opp->getContext(), members, /*isPacked=*/false);
8201 DataLayout dl = DataLayout(opp->getParentOfType<ModuleOp>());
8202 return getTypeByteSize(structType, dl);
8203 }
8204 return 0;
8205}
8206
8207/// Populate default `MinTeams`, `MaxTeams` and `MaxThreads` to their default
8208/// values as stated by the corresponding clauses, if constant.
8209///
8210/// These default values must be set before the creation of the outlined LLVM
8211/// function for the target region, so that they can be used to initialize the
8212/// corresponding global `ConfigurationEnvironmentTy` structure.
8213static void
8214initTargetDefaultAttrs(omp::TargetOp targetOp, Operation *capturedOp,
8215 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::TargetKernelDefaultAttrs &attrs,
8216 bool isTargetDevice, bool isGPU) {
8217 // TODO: Handle constant 'if' clauses.
8218
8219 Value numThreads, numTeamsLower, numTeamsUpper, threadLimit;
8220 if (!isTargetDevice) {
8221 extractHostEvalClauses(targetOp, numThreads, numTeamsLower, numTeamsUpper,
8222 threadLimit);
8223 } else {
8224 // In the target device, values for these clauses are not passed as
8225 // host_eval, but instead evaluated prior to entry to the region. This
8226 // ensures values are mapped and available inside of the target region.
8227 if (auto teamsOp = castOrGetParentOfType<omp::TeamsOp>(capturedOp)) {
8228 numTeamsLower = teamsOp.getNumTeamsLower();
8229 // Handle num_teams upper bounds (only first value for now)
8230 if (!teamsOp.getNumTeamsUpperVars().empty())
8231 numTeamsUpper = teamsOp.getNumTeams(0);
8232 if (!teamsOp.getThreadLimitVars().empty())
8233 threadLimit = teamsOp.getThreadLimit(0);
8234 }
8235
8236 if (auto parallelOp = castOrGetParentOfType<omp::ParallelOp>(capturedOp)) {
8237 if (!parallelOp.getNumThreadsVars().empty())
8238 numThreads = parallelOp.getNumThreads(0);
8239 }
8240 }
8241
8242 // Handle clauses impacting the number of teams.
8243
8244 int32_t minTeamsVal = 1, maxTeamsVal = -1;
8245 if (castOrGetParentOfType<omp::TeamsOp>(capturedOp)) {
8246 // TODO: Use `hostNumTeamsLower` to initialize `minTeamsVal`. For now,
8247 // match clang and set min and max to the same value.
8248 if (numTeamsUpper) {
8249 if (auto val = extractConstInteger(numTeamsUpper))
8250 minTeamsVal = maxTeamsVal = *val;
8251 } else {
8252 minTeamsVal = maxTeamsVal = 0;
8253 }
8254 } else if (castOrGetParentOfType<omp::ParallelOp>(capturedOp,
8255 /*immediateParent=*/true) ||
8256 castOrGetParentOfType<omp::SimdOp>(capturedOp,
8257 /*immediateParent=*/true)) {
8258 minTeamsVal = maxTeamsVal = 1;
8259 } else {
8260 minTeamsVal = maxTeamsVal = -1;
8261 }
8262
8263 // Handle clauses impacting the number of threads.
8264
8265 auto setMaxValueFromClause = [](Value clauseValue, int32_t &result) {
8266 if (!clauseValue)
8267 return;
8268
8269 if (auto val = extractConstInteger(clauseValue))
8270 result = *val;
8271
8272 // Found an applicable clause, so it's not undefined. Mark as unknown
8273 // because it's not constant.
8274 if (result < 0)
8275 result = 0;
8276 };
8277
8278 // Extract 'thread_limit' clause from 'target' and 'teams' directives.
8279 int32_t targetThreadLimitVal = -1, teamsThreadLimitVal = -1;
8280 if (!targetOp.getThreadLimitVars().empty())
8281 setMaxValueFromClause(targetOp.getThreadLimit(0), targetThreadLimitVal);
8282 setMaxValueFromClause(threadLimit, teamsThreadLimitVal);
8283
8284 // Extract 'max_threads' clause from 'parallel' or set to 1 if it's SIMD.
8285 int32_t maxThreadsVal = -1;
8286 if (castOrGetParentOfType<omp::ParallelOp>(capturedOp))
8287 setMaxValueFromClause(numThreads, maxThreadsVal);
8288 else if (castOrGetParentOfType<omp::SimdOp>(capturedOp,
8289 /*immediateParent=*/true))
8290 maxThreadsVal = 1;
8291
8292 // For max values, < 0 means unset, == 0 means set but unknown. Select the
8293 // minimum value between 'max_threads' and 'thread_limit' clauses that were
8294 // set.
8295 int32_t combinedMaxThreadsVal = targetThreadLimitVal;
8296 if (combinedMaxThreadsVal < 0 ||
8297 (teamsThreadLimitVal >= 0 && teamsThreadLimitVal < combinedMaxThreadsVal))
8298 combinedMaxThreadsVal = teamsThreadLimitVal;
8299
8300 if (combinedMaxThreadsVal < 0 ||
8301 (maxThreadsVal >= 0 && maxThreadsVal < combinedMaxThreadsVal))
8302 combinedMaxThreadsVal = maxThreadsVal;
8303
8304 int32_t reductionDataSize = 0;
8305 if (isGPU && capturedOp) {
8306 if (auto teamsOp = castOrGetParentOfType<omp::TeamsOp>(capturedOp))
8307 reductionDataSize = getReductionDataSize(teamsOp);
8308 }
8309
8310 // Update kernel bounds structure for the `OpenMPIRBuilder` to use.
8311 // Use the kernel_type attribute set by the frontend instead of analyzing IR.
8312 omp::TargetExecMode execMode = targetOp.getKernelType();
8313 switch (execMode) {
8314 case omp::TargetExecMode::bare:
8315 attrs.ExecFlags = llvm::omp::OMP_TGT_EXEC_MODE_BARE;
8316 break;
8317 case omp::TargetExecMode::generic:
8318 attrs.ExecFlags = llvm::omp::OMP_TGT_EXEC_MODE_GENERIC;
8319 break;
8320 case omp::TargetExecMode::spmd:
8321 attrs.ExecFlags = llvm::omp::OMP_TGT_EXEC_MODE_SPMD;
8322 break;
8323 case omp::TargetExecMode::spmd_no_loop:
8324 attrs.ExecFlags = llvm::omp::OMP_TGT_EXEC_MODE_SPMD_NO_LOOP;
8325 break;
8326 }
8327 attrs.MinTeams = minTeamsVal;
8328 attrs.MaxTeams.front() = maxTeamsVal;
8329 attrs.MinThreads = 1;
8330 attrs.MaxThreads.front() = combinedMaxThreadsVal;
8331 attrs.ReductionDataSize = reductionDataSize;
8332}
8333
8334/// Gather LLVM runtime values for all clauses evaluated in the host that are
8335/// passed to the kernel invocation.
8336///
8337/// This function must be called only when compiling for the host. Also, it will
8338/// only provide correct results if it's called after the body of \c targetOp
8339/// has been fully generated.
8340static void
8341initTargetRuntimeAttrs(llvm::IRBuilderBase &builder,
8342 LLVM::ModuleTranslation &moduleTranslation,
8343 omp::TargetOp targetOp, Operation *capturedOp,
8344 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::TargetKernelRuntimeAttrs &attrs) {
8345 omp::LoopNestOp loopOp = castOrGetParentOfType<omp::LoopNestOp>(capturedOp);
8346 unsigned numLoops = loopOp ? loopOp.getNumLoops() : 0;
8347
8348 Value numThreads, numTeamsLower, numTeamsUpper, teamsThreadLimit;
8349 llvm::SmallVector<Value> lowerBounds(numLoops), upperBounds(numLoops),
8350 steps(numLoops);
8351 extractHostEvalClauses(targetOp, numThreads, numTeamsLower, numTeamsUpper,
8352 teamsThreadLimit, &lowerBounds, &upperBounds, &steps);
8353
8354 // TODO: Handle constant 'if' clauses.
8355 if (!targetOp.getThreadLimitVars().empty()) {
8356 Value targetThreadLimit = targetOp.getThreadLimit(0);
8357 attrs.TargetThreadLimit.front() =
8358 moduleTranslation.lookupValue(targetThreadLimit);
8359 }
8360
8361 // The __kmpc_push_num_teams_51 function expects int32 as the arguments. So,
8362 // truncate or sign extend lower and upper num_teams bounds as well as
8363 // thread_limit to match int32 ABI requirements for the OpenMP runtime.
8364 if (numTeamsLower)
8365 attrs.MinTeams = builder.CreateSExtOrTrunc(
8366 moduleTranslation.lookupValue(numTeamsLower), builder.getInt32Ty());
8367
8368 if (numTeamsUpper)
8369 attrs.MaxTeams.front() = builder.CreateSExtOrTrunc(
8370 moduleTranslation.lookupValue(numTeamsUpper), builder.getInt32Ty());
8371
8372 if (teamsThreadLimit)
8373 attrs.TeamsThreadLimit.front() = builder.CreateSExtOrTrunc(
8374 moduleTranslation.lookupValue(teamsThreadLimit), builder.getInt32Ty());
8375
8376 if (numThreads)
8377 attrs.MaxThreads = moduleTranslation.lookupValue(numThreads);
8378
8379 if (targetOp.hasHostEvalTripCount()) {
8380 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder *ompBuilder = moduleTranslation.getOpenMPBuilder();
8381 attrs.LoopTripCount = nullptr;
8382
8383 // To calculate the trip count, we multiply together the trip counts of
8384 // every collapsed canonical loop. We don't need to create the loop nests
8385 // here, since we're only interested in the trip count.
8386 for (auto [loopLower, loopUpper, loopStep] :
8387 llvm::zip_equal(lowerBounds, upperBounds, steps)) {
8388 llvm::Value *lowerBound = moduleTranslation.lookupValue(loopLower);
8389 llvm::Value *upperBound = moduleTranslation.lookupValue(loopUpper);
8390 llvm::Value *step = moduleTranslation.lookupValue(loopStep);
8391
8392 if (!lowerBound || !upperBound || !step) {
8393 attrs.LoopTripCount = nullptr;
8394 break;
8395 }
8396
8397 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::LocationDescription loc(builder);
8398 llvm::Value *tripCount = ompBuilder->calculateCanonicalLoopTripCount(
8399 loc, lowerBound, upperBound, step, /*IsSigned=*/true,
8400 loopOp.getLoopInclusive());
8401
8402 if (!attrs.LoopTripCount) {
8403 attrs.LoopTripCount = tripCount;
8404 continue;
8405 }
8406
8407 // TODO: Enable UndefinedSanitizer to diagnose an overflow here.
8408 attrs.LoopTripCount = builder.CreateMul(attrs.LoopTripCount, tripCount,
8409 {}, /*HasNUW=*/true);
8410 }
8411 }
8412
8413 attrs.DeviceID = builder.getInt64(llvm::omp::OMP_DEVICEID_UNDEF);
8414 if (mlir::Value devId = targetOp.getDevice()) {
8415 attrs.DeviceID = moduleTranslation.lookupValue(devId);
8416 attrs.DeviceID =
8417 builder.CreateSExtOrTrunc(attrs.DeviceID, builder.getInt64Ty());
8418 }
8419}
8420
8421static llvm::omp::OMPDynGroupprivateFallbackType
8422getDynGroupprivateFallbackType(omp::FallbackModifierAttr fallbackAttr) {
8423 omp::FallbackModifier fb = fallbackAttr ? fallbackAttr.getValue()
8424 : omp::FallbackModifier::default_mem;
8425 switch (fb) {
8426 case omp::FallbackModifier::abort:
8427 return llvm::omp::OMPDynGroupprivateFallbackType::Abort;
8428 case omp::FallbackModifier::null:
8429 return llvm::omp::OMPDynGroupprivateFallbackType::Null;
8430 case omp::FallbackModifier::default_mem:
8431 return llvm::omp::OMPDynGroupprivateFallbackType::DefaultMem;
8432 }
8433
8434 llvm_unreachable("unexpected dyn_groupprivate fallback type");
8435}
8436
8437static LogicalResult
8438convertOmpTarget(Operation &opInst, llvm::IRBuilderBase &builder,
8439 LLVM::ModuleTranslation &moduleTranslation) {
8440 auto targetOp = cast<omp::TargetOp>(opInst);
8441
8442 // The current debug location already has the DISubprogram for the outlined
8443 // function that will be created for the target op. We save it here so that
8444 // we can set it on the outlined function.
8445 llvm::DebugLoc outlinedFnLoc = builder.getCurrentDebugLocation();
8446 if (failed(checkImplementationStatus(opInst)))
8447 return failure();
8448
8449 // During the handling of target op, we will generate instructions in the
8450 // parent function like call to the oulined function or branch to a new
8451 // BasicBlock. We set the debug location here to parent function so that those
8452 // get the correct debug locations. For outlined functions, the normal MLIR op
8453 // conversion will automatically pick the correct location.
8454 llvm::BasicBlock *parentBB = builder.GetInsertBlock();
8455 assert(parentBB && "No insert block is set for the builder");
8456 llvm::Function *parentLLVMFn = parentBB->getParent();
8457 assert(parentLLVMFn && "Parent Function must be valid");
8458 if (llvm::DISubprogram *SP = parentLLVMFn->getSubprogram())
8459 builder.SetCurrentDebugLocation(llvm::DILocation::get(
8460 parentLLVMFn->getContext(), outlinedFnLoc.getLine(),
8461 outlinedFnLoc.getCol(), SP, outlinedFnLoc.getInlinedAt()));
8462
8463 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder *ompBuilder = moduleTranslation.getOpenMPBuilder();
8464 bool isTargetDevice = ompBuilder->Config.isTargetDevice();
8465 bool isGPU = ompBuilder->Config.isGPU();
8466
8467 auto parentFn = opInst.getParentOfType<LLVM::LLVMFuncOp>();
8468 auto argIface = cast<omp::BlockArgOpenMPOpInterface>(opInst);
8469 auto &targetRegion = targetOp.getRegion();
8470 // Holds the private vars that have been mapped along with the block
8471 // argument that corresponds to the MapInfoOp corresponding to the private
8472 // var in question. So, for instance:
8473 //
8474 // %10 = omp.map.info var_ptr(%6#0 : !fir.ref<!fir.box<!fir.heap<i32>>>, ..)
8475 // omp.target map_entries(%10 -> %arg0) private(@box.privatizer %6#0-> %arg1)
8476 //
8477 // Then, %10 has been created so that the descriptor can be used by the
8478 // privatizer @box.privatizer on the device side. Here we'd record {%6#0,
8479 // %arg0} in the mappedPrivateVars map.
8480 llvm::DenseMap<Value, Value> mappedPrivateVars;
8481 DataLayout dl = DataLayout(opInst.getParentOfType<ModuleOp>());
8482 SmallVector<Value> mapVars = targetOp.getMapVars();
8483 SmallVector<Value> hdaVars = targetOp.getHasDeviceAddrVars();
8484 ArrayRef<BlockArgument> mapBlockArgs = argIface.getMapBlockArgs();
8485 ArrayRef<BlockArgument> hdaBlockArgs = argIface.getHasDeviceAddrBlockArgs();
8486 llvm::Function *llvmOutlinedFn = nullptr;
8487 TargetDirectiveEnumTy targetDirective =
8488 getTargetDirectiveEnumTyFromOp(&opInst);
8489
8490 // TODO: It can also be false if a compile-time constant `false` IF clause is
8491 // specified.
8492 bool isOffloadEntry =
8493 isTargetDevice || !ompBuilder->Config.TargetTriples.empty();
8494
8495 // For some private variables, the MapsForPrivatizedVariablesPass
8496 // creates MapInfoOp instances. Go through the private variables and
8497 // the mapped variables so that during codegeneration we are able
8498 // to quickly look up the corresponding map variable, if any for each
8499 // private variable.
8500 if (!targetOp.getPrivateVars().empty() && !targetOp.getMapVars().empty()) {
8501 OperandRange privateVars = targetOp.getPrivateVars();
8502 std::optional<ArrayAttr> privateSyms = targetOp.getPrivateSyms();
8503 std::optional<DenseI64ArrayAttr> privateMapIndices =
8504 targetOp.getPrivateMapsAttr();
8505
8506 for (auto [privVarIdx, privVarSymPair] :
8507 llvm::enumerate(llvm::zip_equal(privateVars, *privateSyms))) {
8508 auto privVar = std::get<0>(privVarSymPair);
8509 auto privSym = std::get<1>(privVarSymPair);
8510
8511 SymbolRefAttr privatizerName = llvm::cast<SymbolRefAttr>(privSym);
8512 omp::PrivateClauseOp privatizer =
8513 findPrivatizer(targetOp, privatizerName);
8514
8515 if (!privatizer.needsMap())
8516 continue;
8517
8518 mlir::Value mappedValue =
8519 targetOp.getMappedValueForPrivateVar(privVarIdx);
8520 assert(mappedValue && "Expected to find mapped value for a privatized "
8521 "variable that needs mapping");
8522
8523 // The MapInfoOp defining the map var isn't really needed later.
8524 // So, we don't store it in any datastructure. Instead, we just
8525 // do some sanity checks on it right now.
8526 auto mapInfoOp = mappedValue.getDefiningOp<omp::MapInfoOp>();
8527 [[maybe_unused]] Type varType = mapInfoOp.getVarPtrType();
8528
8529 // Check #1: Check that the type of the private variable matches
8530 // the type of the variable being mapped.
8531 if (!isa<LLVM::LLVMPointerType>(privVar.getType()))
8532 assert(
8533 varType == privVar.getType() &&
8534 "Type of private var doesn't match the type of the mapped value");
8535
8536 // Ok, only 1 sanity check for now.
8537 // Record the block argument corresponding to this mapvar.
8538 mappedPrivateVars.insert(
8539 {privVar,
8540 targetRegion.getArgument(argIface.getMapBlockArgsStart() +
8541 (*privateMapIndices)[privVarIdx])});
8542 }
8543 }
8544
8545 using InsertPointTy = llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::InsertPointTy;
8546 auto bodyCB = [&](InsertPointTy allocaIP, InsertPointTy codeGenIP,
8547 ArrayRef<llvm::BasicBlock *> deallocBlocks)
8548 -> llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::InsertPointOrErrorTy {
8549 llvm::IRBuilderBase::InsertPointGuard guard(builder);
8550 builder.SetCurrentDebugLocation(llvm::DebugLoc());
8551 // Forward target-cpu and target-features function attributes from the
8552 // original function to the new outlined function.
8553 llvm::Function *llvmParentFn =
8554 moduleTranslation.lookupFunction(parentFn.getName());
8555 llvmOutlinedFn = codeGenIP.getBlock()->getParent();
8556 assert(llvmParentFn && llvmOutlinedFn &&
8557 "Both parent and outlined functions must exist at this point");
8558
8559 if (outlinedFnLoc && llvmParentFn->getSubprogram())
8560 llvmOutlinedFn->setSubprogram(outlinedFnLoc->getScope()->getSubprogram());
8561
8562 if (auto attr = llvmParentFn->getFnAttribute("target-cpu");
8563 attr.isStringAttribute())
8564 llvmOutlinedFn->addFnAttr(attr);
8565
8566 if (auto attr = llvmParentFn->getFnAttribute("target-features");
8567 attr.isStringAttribute())
8568 llvmOutlinedFn->addFnAttr(attr);
8569
8570 for (auto [arg, mapOp] : llvm::zip_equal(mapBlockArgs, mapVars)) {
8571 auto mapInfoOp = cast<omp::MapInfoOp>(mapOp.getDefiningOp());
8572 llvm::Value *mapOpValue =
8573 moduleTranslation.lookupValue(mapInfoOp.getVarPtr());
8574 moduleTranslation.mapValue(arg, mapOpValue);
8575 }
8576 for (auto [arg, mapOp] : llvm::zip_equal(hdaBlockArgs, hdaVars)) {
8577 auto mapInfoOp = cast<omp::MapInfoOp>(mapOp.getDefiningOp());
8578 llvm::Value *mapOpValue =
8579 moduleTranslation.lookupValue(mapInfoOp.getVarPtr());
8580 moduleTranslation.mapValue(arg, mapOpValue);
8581 }
8582
8583 // Do privatization after moduleTranslation has already recorded
8584 // mapped values.
8585 PrivateVarsInfo privateVarsInfo(targetOp);
8586
8587 llvm::Expected<llvm::BasicBlock *> afterAllocas =
8588 allocatePrivateVars(targetOp, builder, moduleTranslation,
8589 privateVarsInfo, allocaIP, &mappedPrivateVars);
8590
8591 if (failed(handleError(afterAllocas, *targetOp)))
8592 return llvm::make_error<PreviouslyReportedError>();
8593
8594 builder.restoreIP(codeGenIP);
8595 if (handleError(initPrivateVars(builder, moduleTranslation, privateVarsInfo,
8596 &mappedPrivateVars),
8597 *targetOp)
8598 .failed())
8599 return llvm::make_error<PreviouslyReportedError>();
8600
8601 if (failed(copyFirstPrivateVars(
8602 targetOp, builder, moduleTranslation, privateVarsInfo.mlirVars,
8603 privateVarsInfo.llvmVars, privateVarsInfo.privatizers,
8604 targetOp.getPrivateNeedsBarrier(), &mappedPrivateVars)))
8605 return llvm::make_error<PreviouslyReportedError>();
8606
8607 LLVM::ModuleTranslation::SaveStack<OpenMPAllocStackFrame> frame(
8608 moduleTranslation, allocaIP, deallocBlocks);
8609 llvm::Expected<llvm::BasicBlock *> exitBlock = convertOmpOpRegions(
8610 targetRegion, "omp.target", builder, moduleTranslation);
8611
8612 if (failed(handleError(exitBlock, *targetOp)))
8613 return llvm::make_error<PreviouslyReportedError>();
8614
8615 builder.SetInsertPoint(exitBlock.get()->getTerminator());
8616
8617 if (failed(cleanupPrivateVars(targetOp, builder, moduleTranslation,
8618 targetOp.getLoc(), privateVarsInfo)))
8619 return llvm::make_error<PreviouslyReportedError>();
8620
8621 return builder.saveIP();
8622 };
8623
8624 StringRef parentName = parentFn.getName();
8625
8626 llvm::TargetRegionEntryInfo entryInfo;
8627
8628 getTargetEntryUniqueInfo(entryInfo, targetOp,
8629 *moduleTranslation.getOpenMPBuilder(),
8630 moduleTranslation.getFileSystem(), parentName);
8631
8632 MapInfoData mapData;
8633 collectMapDataFromMapOperands(mapData, mapVars, moduleTranslation, dl,
8634 builder, /*useDevPtrOperands=*/{},
8635 /*useDevAddrOperands=*/{}, hdaVars);
8636
8637 MapInfosTy combinedInfos;
8638 auto genMapInfoCB =
8639 [&](llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::InsertPointTy codeGenIP) -> MapInfosTy & {
8640 builder.restoreIP(codeGenIP);
8641 genMapInfos(builder, moduleTranslation, dl, combinedInfos, mapData,
8642 targetDirective);
8643
8644 // Append a null entry for the implicit dyn_ptr argument so the argument
8645 // count sent to the runtime already includes it.
8646 auto *nullPtr = llvm::Constant::getNullValue(builder.getPtrTy());
8647 combinedInfos.BasePointers.push_back(nullPtr);
8648 combinedInfos.Pointers.push_back(nullPtr);
8649 combinedInfos.DevicePointers.push_back(
8650 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::DeviceInfoTy::None);
8651 combinedInfos.Sizes.push_back(builder.getInt64(0));
8652 combinedInfos.Types.push_back(
8653 llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags::OMP_MAP_TARGET_PARAM |
8654 llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags::OMP_MAP_LITERAL);
8655 if (!combinedInfos.Names.empty())
8656 combinedInfos.Names.push_back(nullPtr);
8657 combinedInfos.Mappers.push_back(nullptr);
8658
8659 return combinedInfos;
8660 };
8661
8662 auto argAccessorCB = [&](llvm::Argument &arg, llvm::Value *input,
8663 llvm::Value *&retVal, InsertPointTy allocaIP,
8664 InsertPointTy codeGenIP,
8665 llvm::ArrayRef<InsertPointTy> deallocIPs)
8666 -> llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::InsertPointOrErrorTy {
8667 llvm::IRBuilderBase::InsertPointGuard guard(builder);
8668 builder.SetCurrentDebugLocation(llvm::DebugLoc());
8669 // We just return the unaltered argument for the host function
8670 // for now, some alterations may be required in the future to
8671 // keep host fallback functions working identically to the device
8672 // version (e.g. pass ByCopy values should be treated as such on
8673 // host and device, currently not always the case)
8674 if (!isTargetDevice) {
8675 retVal = cast<llvm::Value>(&arg);
8676 return codeGenIP;
8677 }
8678
8679 return createDeviceArgumentAccessor(targetOp, mapData, arg, input, retVal,
8680 builder, *ompBuilder, moduleTranslation,
8681 allocaIP, codeGenIP, deallocIPs);
8682 };
8683
8684 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::TargetKernelRuntimeAttrs runtimeAttrs;
8685 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::TargetKernelDefaultAttrs defaultAttrs;
8686 Operation *targetCapturedOp =
8687 cast<omp::ComposableOpInterface>(*targetOp).findCapturedOp();
8688 initTargetDefaultAttrs(targetOp, targetCapturedOp, defaultAttrs,
8689 isTargetDevice, isGPU);
8690
8691 // Collect host-evaluated values needed to properly launch the kernel from the
8692 // host.
8693 if (!isTargetDevice)
8694 initTargetRuntimeAttrs(builder, moduleTranslation, targetOp,
8695 targetCapturedOp, runtimeAttrs);
8696
8697 // Pass host-evaluated values as parameters to the kernel / host fallback,
8698 // except if they are constants. In any case, map the MLIR block argument to
8699 // the corresponding LLVM values.
8700 llvm::SmallVector<llvm::Value *, 4> kernelInput;
8701 SmallVector<Value> hostEvalVars = targetOp.getHostEvalVars();
8702 ArrayRef<BlockArgument> hostEvalBlockArgs = argIface.getHostEvalBlockArgs();
8703 for (auto [arg, var] : llvm::zip_equal(hostEvalBlockArgs, hostEvalVars)) {
8704 llvm::Value *value = moduleTranslation.lookupValue(var);
8705 moduleTranslation.mapValue(arg, value);
8706
8707 if (!llvm::isa<llvm::Constant>(value))
8708 kernelInput.push_back(value);
8709 }
8710
8711 for (size_t i = 0, e = mapData.OriginalValue.size(); i != e; ++i) {
8712 // 1) Declare target arguments are not passed to kernels as arguments.
8713 // 2) Attach maps are not passed in as arguments to kernels.
8714 // 3) Children of record objects are not passed in as arguments.
8715 // TODO: We currently do not handle cases where a member is explicitly
8716 // passed in as an argument, this will likley need to be handled in
8717 // the near future, rather than using IsAMember, it may be better to
8718 // test if the relevant BlockArg is used within the target region and
8719 // then use that as a basis for exclusion in the kernel inputs.
8720 bool isAttachMap = (mapData.Types[i] &
8721 llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags::OMP_MAP_ATTACH) ==
8722 llvm::omp::OpenMPOffloadMappingFlags::OMP_MAP_ATTACH;
8723 if (!mapData.IsDeclareTarget[i] && !mapData.IsAMember[i] && !isAttachMap)
8724 kernelInput.push_back(mapData.OriginalValue[i]);
8725 }
8726
8727 llvm::SmallVector<llvm::BasicBlock *> deallocBlocks;
8728 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::InsertPointTy allocaIP =
8729 findAllocInsertPoints(builder, moduleTranslation, &deallocBlocks);
8730
8731 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::DependenciesInfo dds;
8732 if (failed(buildDependData(
8733 targetOp.getDependVars(), targetOp.getDependKinds(),
8734 targetOp.getDependIterated(), targetOp.getDependIteratedKinds(),
8735 builder, moduleTranslation, dds)))
8736 return failure();
8737
8738 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::LocationDescription ompLoc(builder);
8739
8740 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::TargetDataInfo info(
8741 /*RequiresDevicePointerInfo=*/false,
8742 /*SeparateBeginEndCalls=*/true);
8743
8744 auto customMapperCB =
8745 [&](unsigned int i) -> llvm::Expected<llvm::Function *> {
8746 if (!combinedInfos.Mappers[i])
8747 return nullptr;
8748 info.HasMapper = true;
8749 return getOrCreateUserDefinedMapperFunc(combinedInfos.Mappers[i], builder,
8750 moduleTranslation, targetDirective);
8751 };
8752
8753 llvm::Value *ifCond = nullptr;
8754 if (Value targetIfCond = targetOp.getIfExpr())
8755 ifCond = moduleTranslation.lookupValue(targetIfCond);
8756
8757 Value dynGroupPrivateSize = targetOp.getDynGroupprivateSize();
8758 llvm::Value *dynSizeVal = nullptr;
8759 if (dynGroupPrivateSize) {
8760 dynSizeVal = moduleTranslation.lookupValue(dynGroupPrivateSize);
8761 dynSizeVal = builder.CreateIntCast(dynSizeVal, builder.getInt32Ty(),
8762 /*isSigned=*/false);
8763 }
8764
8765 llvm::omp::OMPDynGroupprivateFallbackType fallbackType =
8766 getDynGroupprivateFallbackType(targetOp.getDynGroupprivateFallbackAttr());
8767
8768 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::InsertPointOrErrorTy afterIP =
8769 moduleTranslation.getOpenMPBuilder()->createTarget(
8770 ompLoc, isOffloadEntry, allocaIP, builder.saveIP(), deallocBlocks,
8771 info, entryInfo, defaultAttrs, runtimeAttrs, ifCond, kernelInput,
8772 genMapInfoCB, bodyCB, argAccessorCB, customMapperCB, dds,
8773 targetOp.getNowait(), dynSizeVal, fallbackType);
8774
8775 if (failed(handleError(afterIP, opInst)))
8776 return failure();
8777
8778 builder.restoreIP(*afterIP);
8779
8780 if (dds.DepArray)
8781 builder.CreateFree(dds.DepArray);
8782
8783 // Remap access operations to declare target reference pointers for the
8784 // device, essentially generating extra loadop's as necessary
8785 if (moduleTranslation.getOpenMPBuilder()->Config.isTargetDevice())
8786 handleDeclareTargetMapVar(mapData, moduleTranslation, builder,
8787 llvmOutlinedFn);
8788
8789 return success();
8790}
8791
8792static LogicalResult
8793convertDeclareTargetAttr(Operation *op, mlir::omp::DeclareTargetAttr attribute,
8794 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder *ompBuilder,
8795 LLVM::ModuleTranslation &moduleTranslation) {
8796 // Amend omp.declare_target by deleting the IR of the outlined functions
8797 // created for target regions. They cannot be filtered out from MLIR earlier
8798 // because the omp.target operation inside must be translated to LLVM, but
8799 // the wrapper functions themselves must not remain at the end of the
8800 // process. We know that functions where omp.declare_target does not match
8801 // omp.is_target_device at this stage can only be wrapper functions because
8802 // those that aren't are removed earlier as an MLIR transformation pass.
8803 if (FunctionOpInterface funcOp = dyn_cast<FunctionOpInterface>(op)) {
8804 if (auto offloadMod = dyn_cast<omp::OffloadModuleInterface>(
8805 op->getParentOfType<ModuleOp>().getOperation())) {
8806 if (!offloadMod.getIsTargetDevice())
8807 return success();
8808
8809 omp::DeclareTargetDeviceType declareType =
8810 attribute.getDeviceType().getValue();
8811
8812 if (declareType == omp::DeclareTargetDeviceType::host) {
8813 llvm::Function *llvmFunc =
8814 moduleTranslation.lookupFunction(funcOp.getName());
8815 llvmFunc->dropAllReferences();
8816 llvmFunc->eraseFromParent();
8817
8818 // Invalidate the builder's current insertion point, as it now points to
8819 // a deleted block.
8820 ompBuilder->Builder.ClearInsertionPoint();
8821 ompBuilder->Builder.SetCurrentDebugLocation(llvm::DebugLoc());
8822 }
8823 }
8824 return success();
8825 }
8826
8827 if (LLVM::GlobalOp gOp = dyn_cast<LLVM::GlobalOp>(op)) {
8828 llvm::Module *llvmModule = moduleTranslation.getLLVMModule();
8829 if (auto *gVal = llvmModule->getNamedValue(gOp.getSymName())) {
8830 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder *ompBuilder = moduleTranslation.getOpenMPBuilder();
8831 bool isDeclaration = gOp.isDeclaration();
8832 bool isExternallyVisible =
8833 gOp.getVisibility() != mlir::SymbolTable::Visibility::Private;
8834 auto loc = op->getLoc()->findInstanceOf<FileLineColLoc>();
8835 llvm::StringRef mangledName = gOp.getSymName();
8836 auto captureClause =
8837 convertToCaptureClauseKind(attribute.getCaptureClause().getValue());
8838 auto deviceClause =
8839 convertToDeviceClauseKind(attribute.getDeviceType().getValue());
8840 // unused for MLIR at the moment, required in Clang for book
8841 // keeping
8842 std::vector<llvm::GlobalVariable *> generatedRefs;
8843
8844 std::vector<llvm::Triple> targetTriple;
8845 auto targetTripleAttr = dyn_cast_or_null<mlir::StringAttr>(
8846 op->getParentOfType<mlir::ModuleOp>()->getAttr(
8847 LLVM::LLVMDialect::getTargetTripleAttrName()));
8848 if (targetTripleAttr)
8849 targetTriple.emplace_back(targetTripleAttr.data());
8850
8851 auto fileInfoCallBack = [&loc]() {
8852 std::string filename = "";
8853 std::uint64_t lineNo = 0;
8854
8855 if (loc) {
8856 filename = loc.getFilename().str();
8857 lineNo = loc.getLine();
8858 }
8859
8860 return std::pair<std::string, std::uint64_t>(llvm::StringRef(filename),
8861 lineNo);
8862 };
8863
8864 llvm::vfs::FileSystem &vfs = moduleTranslation.getFileSystem();
8865
8866 ompBuilder->registerTargetGlobalVariable(
8867 captureClause, deviceClause, isDeclaration, isExternallyVisible,
8868 ompBuilder->getTargetEntryUniqueInfo(fileInfoCallBack, vfs),
8869 mangledName, generatedRefs, /*OpenMPSimd*/ false, targetTriple,
8870 /*GlobalInitializer*/ nullptr, /*VariableLinkage*/ nullptr,
8871 gVal->getType(), gVal);
8872
8873 bool requiresUSM = ompBuilder->Config.hasRequiresUnifiedSharedMemory();
8874 if (ompBuilder->Config.isTargetDevice() &&
8875 (attribute.getCaptureClause().getValue() ==
8876 mlir::omp::DeclareTargetCaptureClause::link ||
8877 requiresUSM)) {
8878 llvm::Type *ptrTy = gVal->getType();
8879 // For USM the global type becomes a pointer handle, as opposed to the
8880 // globals original type.
8881 if (requiresUSM)
8882 ptrTy = llvm::PointerType::get(llvmModule->getContext(), 0);
8883 ompBuilder->getAddrOfDeclareTargetVar(
8884 captureClause, deviceClause, isDeclaration, isExternallyVisible,
8885 ompBuilder->getTargetEntryUniqueInfo(fileInfoCallBack, vfs),
8886 mangledName, generatedRefs, /*OpenMPSimd*/ false, targetTriple,
8887 ptrTy, /*GlobalInitializer*/ nullptr,
8888 /*VariableLinkage*/ nullptr);
8889 }
8890 }
8891 }
8892
8893 return success();
8894}
8895
8896namespace {
8897
8898/// Implementation of the dialect interface that converts operations belonging
8899/// to the OpenMP dialect to LLVM IR.
8900class OpenMPDialectLLVMIRTranslationInterface
8901 : public LLVMTranslationDialectInterface {
8902public:
8903 using LLVMTranslationDialectInterface::LLVMTranslationDialectInterface;
8904
8905 /// Translates the given operation to LLVM IR using the provided IR builder
8906 /// and saving the state in `moduleTranslation`.
8907 LogicalResult
8908 convertOperation(Operation *op, llvm::IRBuilderBase &builder,
8909 LLVM::ModuleTranslation &moduleTranslation) const final;
8910
8911 /// Given an OpenMP MLIR attribute, create the corresponding LLVM-IR,
8912 /// runtime calls, or operation amendments
8913 LogicalResult
8914 amendOperation(Operation *op, ArrayRef<llvm::Instruction *> instructions,
8915 NamedAttribute attribute,
8916 LLVM::ModuleTranslation &moduleTranslation) const final;
8917
8918 /// Records the LLVM alloc pointer produced for an OMP ALLOCATE variable so
8919 /// that the paired omp.allocate_free op can generate the matching
8920 /// __kmpc_free call.
8921 void registerAllocatedPtr(Value var, llvm::Value *ptr) const {
8922 ompAllocatedPtrs[var] = ptr;
8923 }
8924
8925 /// Returns the LLVM alloc pointer previously registered for var, or
8926 /// nullptr if no allocation was recorded.
8927 llvm::Value *lookupAllocatedPtr(Value var) const {
8928 auto it = ompAllocatedPtrs.find(var);
8929 return it != ompAllocatedPtrs.end() ? it->second : nullptr;
8930 }
8931
8932private:
8933 /// Maps each MLIR variable value that appeared in an omp.allocate_dir op to
8934 /// the LLVM pointer returned by the corresponding __kmpc_alloc call. The
8935 /// paired omp.allocate_free op looks up these pointers to emit __kmpc_free.
8936 mutable DenseMap<Value, llvm::Value *> ompAllocatedPtrs;
8937};
8938
8939} // namespace
8940
8941LogicalResult OpenMPDialectLLVMIRTranslationInterface::amendOperation(
8942 Operation *op, ArrayRef<llvm::Instruction *> instructions,
8943 NamedAttribute attribute,
8944 LLVM::ModuleTranslation &moduleTranslation) const {
8945 return llvm::StringSwitch<llvm::function_ref<LogicalResult(Attribute)>>(
8946 attribute.getName())
8947 .Case("omp.is_target_device",
8948 [&](Attribute attr) {
8949 if (auto deviceAttr = dyn_cast<BoolAttr>(attr)) {
8950 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilderConfig &config =
8951 moduleTranslation.getOpenMPBuilder()->Config;
8952 config.setIsTargetDevice(deviceAttr.getValue());
8953 return success();
8954 }
8955 return failure();
8956 })
8957 .Case("omp.is_gpu",
8958 [&](Attribute attr) {
8959 if (auto gpuAttr = dyn_cast<BoolAttr>(attr)) {
8960 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilderConfig &config =
8961 moduleTranslation.getOpenMPBuilder()->Config;
8962 config.setIsGPU(gpuAttr.getValue());
8963 return success();
8964 }
8965 return failure();
8966 })
8967 .Case("omp.host_ir_filepath",
8968 [&](Attribute attr) {
8969 if (auto filepathAttr = dyn_cast<StringAttr>(attr)) {
8970 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder *ompBuilder =
8971 moduleTranslation.getOpenMPBuilder();
8972 ompBuilder->loadOffloadInfoMetadata(
8973 moduleTranslation.getFileSystem(), filepathAttr.getValue());
8974 return success();
8975 }
8976 return failure();
8977 })
8978 .Case("omp.flags",
8979 [&](Attribute attr) {
8980 if (auto rtlAttr = dyn_cast<omp::FlagsAttr>(attr))
8981 return convertFlagsAttr(op, rtlAttr, moduleTranslation);
8982 return failure();
8983 })
8984 .Case("omp.version",
8985 [&](Attribute attr) {
8986 if (auto versionAttr = dyn_cast<omp::VersionAttr>(attr)) {
8987 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder *ompBuilder =
8988 moduleTranslation.getOpenMPBuilder();
8989 ompBuilder->M.addModuleFlag(llvm::Module::Max, "openmp",
8990 versionAttr.getVersion());
8991 return success();
8992 }
8993 return failure();
8994 })
8995 .Case("omp.declare_target",
8996 [&](Attribute attr) {
8997 if (auto declareTargetAttr =
8998 dyn_cast<omp::DeclareTargetAttr>(attr)) {
8999 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder *ompBuilder =
9000 moduleTranslation.getOpenMPBuilder();
9001 return convertDeclareTargetAttr(op, declareTargetAttr,
9002 ompBuilder, moduleTranslation);
9003 }
9004 return failure();
9005 })
9006 .Case("omp.requires",
9007 [&](Attribute attr) {
9008 if (auto requiresAttr = dyn_cast<omp::ClauseRequiresAttr>(attr)) {
9009 using Requires = omp::ClauseRequires;
9010 Requires flags = requiresAttr.getValue();
9011 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilderConfig &config =
9012 moduleTranslation.getOpenMPBuilder()->Config;
9013 config.setHasRequiresReverseOffload(
9014 bitEnumContainsAll(flags, Requires::reverse_offload));
9015 config.setHasRequiresUnifiedAddress(
9016 bitEnumContainsAll(flags, Requires::unified_address));
9017 config.setHasRequiresUnifiedSharedMemory(
9018 bitEnumContainsAll(flags, Requires::unified_shared_memory));
9019 config.setHasRequiresDynamicAllocators(
9020 bitEnumContainsAll(flags, Requires::dynamic_allocators));
9021 return success();
9022 }
9023 return failure();
9024 })
9025 .Case("omp.target_triples",
9026 [&](Attribute attr) {
9027 if (auto triplesAttr = dyn_cast<ArrayAttr>(attr)) {
9028 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilderConfig &config =
9029 moduleTranslation.getOpenMPBuilder()->Config;
9030 config.TargetTriples.clear();
9031 config.TargetTriples.reserve(triplesAttr.size());
9032 for (Attribute tripleAttr : triplesAttr) {
9033 if (auto tripleStrAttr = dyn_cast<StringAttr>(tripleAttr))
9034 config.TargetTriples.emplace_back(tripleStrAttr.getValue());
9035 else
9036 return failure();
9037 }
9038 return success();
9039 }
9040 return failure();
9041 })
9042 .Default([](Attribute) {
9043 // Fall through for omp attributes that do not require lowering.
9044 return success();
9045 })(attribute.getValue());
9046
9047 return failure();
9048}
9049
9050// Returns true if the operation is not inside a TargetOp, it is part of a
9051// function and that function is not declare target.
9052static bool isHostDeviceOp(Operation *op) {
9053 // Assumes no reverse offloading
9054 if (op->getParentOfType<omp::TargetOp>())
9055 return false;
9056
9057 if (auto parentFn = op->getParentOfType<LLVM::LLVMFuncOp>()) {
9058 if (auto declareTargetIface =
9059 llvm::dyn_cast<mlir::omp::DeclareTargetInterface>(
9060 parentFn.getOperation()))
9061 if (declareTargetIface.isDeclareTarget() &&
9062 declareTargetIface.getDeclareTargetDeviceType() !=
9063 mlir::omp::DeclareTargetDeviceType::host)
9064 return false;
9065
9066 return true;
9067 }
9068
9069 return false;
9070}
9071
9072static llvm::Function *getOmpTargetAlloc(llvm::IRBuilderBase &builder,
9073 llvm::Module *llvmModule) {
9074 llvm::Type *i64Ty = builder.getInt64Ty();
9075 llvm::Type *i32Ty = builder.getInt32Ty();
9076 llvm::Type *returnType = builder.getPtrTy(0);
9077 llvm::FunctionType *fnType =
9078 llvm::FunctionType::get(returnType, {i64Ty, i32Ty}, false);
9079 llvm::Function *func = cast<llvm::Function>(
9080 llvmModule->getOrInsertFunction("omp_target_alloc", fnType).getCallee());
9081 return func;
9082}
9083
9084template <typename T>
9085static llvm::Value *
9086getAllocationSize(llvm::IRBuilderBase &builder,
9087 LLVM::ModuleTranslation &moduleTranslation, T op) {
9088 llvm::DataLayout dataLayout =
9089 moduleTranslation.getLLVMModule()->getDataLayout();
9090 llvm::Type *llvmHeapTy =
9091 moduleTranslation.convertType(op.getMemElemTypeAttr().getValue());
9092
9093 auto alignment = op.getMemAlignment();
9094 llvm::TypeSize typeSize = llvm::alignTo(
9095 dataLayout.getTypeStoreSize(llvmHeapTy),
9096 alignment ? *alignment : dataLayout.getABITypeAlign(llvmHeapTy).value());
9097
9098 llvm::Value *allocSize = builder.getInt64(typeSize.getFixedValue());
9099 return builder.CreateMul(
9100 allocSize,
9101 builder.CreateIntCast(moduleTranslation.lookupValue(op.getMemArraySize()),
9102 builder.getInt64Ty(),
9103 /*isSigned=*/false));
9104}
9105
9106template <>
9107llvm::Value *getAllocationSize(llvm::IRBuilderBase &builder,
9108 LLVM::ModuleTranslation &moduleTranslation,
9109 omp::TargetAllocMemOp op) {
9110 llvm::DataLayout dataLayout =
9111 moduleTranslation.getLLVMModule()->getDataLayout();
9112 llvm::Type *llvmHeapTy = moduleTranslation.convertType(op.getAllocatedType());
9113 llvm::TypeSize typeSize = dataLayout.getTypeAllocSize(llvmHeapTy);
9114 llvm::Value *allocSize = builder.getInt64(typeSize.getFixedValue());
9115 for (auto typeParam : op.getTypeparams()) {
9116 allocSize = builder.CreateMul(
9117 allocSize,
9118 builder.CreateIntCast(moduleTranslation.lookupValue(typeParam),
9119 builder.getInt64Ty(),
9120 /*isSigned=*/false));
9121 }
9122 return allocSize;
9123}
9124
9125static LogicalResult
9126convertTargetAllocMemOp(Operation &opInst, llvm::IRBuilderBase &builder,
9127 LLVM::ModuleTranslation &moduleTranslation) {
9128 auto allocMemOp = cast<omp::TargetAllocMemOp>(opInst);
9129 if (!allocMemOp)
9130 return failure();
9131
9132 // Get "omp_target_alloc" function
9133 llvm::Module *llvmModule = moduleTranslation.getLLVMModule();
9134 llvm::Function *ompTargetAllocFunc = getOmpTargetAlloc(builder, llvmModule);
9135 // Get the corresponding device value in llvm
9136 mlir::Value deviceNum = allocMemOp.getDevice();
9137 llvm::Value *llvmDeviceNum = moduleTranslation.lookupValue(deviceNum);
9138 // Get the allocation size.
9139 llvm::Value *allocSize =
9140 getAllocationSize(builder, moduleTranslation, allocMemOp);
9141 // Create call to "omp_target_alloc" with the args as translated llvm values.
9142 llvm::CallInst *call =
9143 builder.CreateCall(ompTargetAllocFunc, {allocSize, llvmDeviceNum});
9144 llvm::Value *resultI64 = builder.CreatePtrToInt(call, builder.getInt64Ty());
9145
9146 // Map the result
9147 moduleTranslation.mapValue(allocMemOp.getResult(), resultI64);
9148 return success();
9149}
9150
9151static LogicalResult
9152convertAllocSharedMemOp(omp::AllocSharedMemOp allocMemOp,
9153 llvm::IRBuilderBase &builder,
9154 LLVM::ModuleTranslation &moduleTranslation) {
9155 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder *ompBuilder = moduleTranslation.getOpenMPBuilder();
9156 llvm::Value *size = getAllocationSize(builder, moduleTranslation, allocMemOp);
9157 moduleTranslation.mapValue(allocMemOp.getResult(),
9158 ompBuilder->createOMPAllocShared(builder, size));
9159 return success();
9160}
9161
9162static LogicalResult
9163convertAllocateDirOp(Operation &opInst, llvm::IRBuilderBase &builder,
9164 LLVM::ModuleTranslation &moduleTranslation,
9165 const OpenMPDialectLLVMIRTranslationInterface &ompIface) {
9166 auto allocateDirOp = cast<omp::AllocateDirOp>(opInst);
9167 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder *ompBuilder = moduleTranslation.getOpenMPBuilder();
9168
9169 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::LocationDescription ompLoc(builder);
9170 llvm::Module *llvmModule = moduleTranslation.getLLVMModule();
9171 llvm::DataLayout dataLayout = llvmModule->getDataLayout();
9172 SmallVector<Value> vars = allocateDirOp.getVarList();
9173 std::optional<int64_t> alignAttr = allocateDirOp.getAlign();
9174
9175 llvm::Value *allocator;
9176 if (auto allocatorVar = allocateDirOp.getAllocator()) {
9177 allocator = moduleTranslation.lookupValue(allocatorVar);
9178 if (allocator->getType()->isIntegerTy())
9179 allocator = builder.CreateIntToPtr(allocator, builder.getPtrTy());
9180 else if (allocator->getType()->isPointerTy())
9181 allocator = builder.CreatePointerBitCastOrAddrSpaceCast(
9182 allocator, builder.getPtrTy());
9183 } else {
9184 allocator = llvm::ConstantPointerNull::get(builder.getPtrTy());
9185 }
9186
9187 for (Value var : vars) {
9188 llvm::Type *llvmVarTy = moduleTranslation.convertType(var.getType());
9189
9190 // Opaque pointers lose element type. Trace to GlobalOp for type
9191 // Falls back to llvmVarTy when not from a global.
9192 llvm::Type *typeToInspect = llvmVarTy;
9193 if (llvmVarTy->isPointerTy()) {
9194 Value baseVar = getBaseValueForTypeLookup(var);
9195 if (Operation *globalOp = getGlobalOpFromValue(baseVar)) {
9196 if (auto gop = dyn_cast<LLVM::GlobalOp>(globalOp))
9197 typeToInspect = moduleTranslation.convertType(gop.getGlobalType());
9198 }
9199 }
9200
9201 llvm::Value *size;
9202 if (auto arrTy = llvm::dyn_cast<llvm::ArrayType>(typeToInspect)) {
9203 llvm::Value *elementCount = builder.getInt64(1);
9204 llvm::Type *currentType = arrTy;
9205 while (auto nestedArrTy = llvm::dyn_cast<llvm::ArrayType>(currentType)) {
9206 elementCount = builder.CreateMul(
9207 elementCount, builder.getInt64(nestedArrTy->getNumElements()));
9208 currentType = nestedArrTy->getElementType();
9209 }
9210 uint64_t elemSizeInBits = dataLayout.getTypeSizeInBits(currentType);
9211 size =
9212 builder.CreateMul(elementCount, builder.getInt64(elemSizeInBits / 8));
9213 } else {
9214 size = builder.getInt64(
9215 dataLayout.getTypeStoreSize(typeToInspect).getFixedValue());
9216 }
9217
9218 uint64_t alignValue =
9219 alignAttr ? alignAttr.value()
9220 : dataLayout.getABITypeAlign(typeToInspect).value();
9221 llvm::Value *alignConst = builder.getInt64(alignValue);
9222 // Align the size: ((size + align - 1) / align) * align
9223 size = builder.CreateAdd(size, builder.getInt64(alignValue - 1), "", true);
9224 size = builder.CreateUDiv(size, alignConst);
9225 size = builder.CreateMul(size, alignConst, "", true);
9226
9227 std::string allocName =
9228 ompBuilder->createPlatformSpecificName({".void.addr"});
9229 llvm::CallInst *allocCall;
9230 if (alignAttr.has_value()) {
9231 allocCall = ompBuilder->createOMPAlignedAlloc(
9232 ompLoc, builder.getInt64(alignAttr.value()), size, allocator,
9233 allocName);
9234 } else {
9235 allocCall =
9236 ompBuilder->createOMPAlloc(ompLoc, size, allocator, allocName);
9237 }
9238 // Record the alloc pointer keyed by the MLIR variable value.
9239 ompIface.registerAllocatedPtr(var, allocCall);
9240 }
9241
9242 return success();
9243}
9244
9245static LogicalResult
9246convertAllocateFreeOp(Operation &opInst, llvm::IRBuilderBase &builder,
9247 LLVM::ModuleTranslation &moduleTranslation,
9248 const OpenMPDialectLLVMIRTranslationInterface &ompIface) {
9249 auto freeOp = cast<omp::AllocateFreeOp>(opInst);
9250 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder *ompBuilder = moduleTranslation.getOpenMPBuilder();
9251 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::LocationDescription ompLoc(builder);
9252
9253 llvm::Value *allocator;
9254 if (auto allocatorVar = freeOp.getAllocator()) {
9255 allocator = moduleTranslation.lookupValue(allocatorVar);
9256 if (allocator->getType()->isIntegerTy())
9257 allocator = builder.CreateIntToPtr(allocator, builder.getPtrTy());
9258 else if (allocator->getType()->isPointerTy())
9259 allocator = builder.CreatePointerBitCastOrAddrSpaceCast(
9260 allocator, builder.getPtrTy());
9261 } else {
9262 allocator = llvm::ConstantPointerNull::get(builder.getPtrTy());
9263 }
9264
9265 // Emit __kmpc_free for each variable in reverse allocation order.
9266 SmallVector<Value> vars = freeOp.getVarList();
9267 for (Value var : llvm::reverse(vars)) {
9268 llvm::Value *allocPtr = ompIface.lookupAllocatedPtr(var);
9269 if (!allocPtr)
9270 return opInst.emitError("omp.allocate_free: no allocation recorded");
9271 ompBuilder->createOMPFree(ompLoc, allocPtr, allocator, "");
9272 }
9273
9274 return success();
9275}
9276
9277static llvm::Function *getOmpTargetFree(llvm::IRBuilderBase &builder,
9278 llvm::Module *llvmModule) {
9279 llvm::Type *ptrTy = builder.getPtrTy(0);
9280 llvm::Type *i32Ty = builder.getInt32Ty();
9281 llvm::Type *voidTy = builder.getVoidTy();
9282 llvm::FunctionType *fnType =
9283 llvm::FunctionType::get(voidTy, {ptrTy, i32Ty}, false);
9284 llvm::Function *func = dyn_cast<llvm::Function>(
9285 llvmModule->getOrInsertFunction("omp_target_free", fnType).getCallee());
9286 return func;
9287}
9288
9289static LogicalResult
9290convertTargetFreeMemOp(Operation &opInst, llvm::IRBuilderBase &builder,
9291 LLVM::ModuleTranslation &moduleTranslation) {
9292 auto freeMemOp = cast<omp::TargetFreeMemOp>(opInst);
9293 if (!freeMemOp)
9294 return failure();
9295
9296 // Get "omp_target_free" function
9297 llvm::Module *llvmModule = moduleTranslation.getLLVMModule();
9298 llvm::Function *ompTragetFreeFunc = getOmpTargetFree(builder, llvmModule);
9299 // Get the corresponding device value in llvm
9300 mlir::Value deviceNum = freeMemOp.getDevice();
9301 llvm::Value *llvmDeviceNum = moduleTranslation.lookupValue(deviceNum);
9302 // Get the corresponding heapref value in llvm
9303 mlir::Value heapref = freeMemOp.getHeapref();
9304 llvm::Value *llvmHeapref = moduleTranslation.lookupValue(heapref);
9305 // Convert heapref int to ptr and call "omp_target_free"
9306 llvm::Value *intToPtr =
9307 builder.CreateIntToPtr(llvmHeapref, builder.getPtrTy(0));
9308 builder.CreateCall(ompTragetFreeFunc, {intToPtr, llvmDeviceNum});
9309 return success();
9310}
9311
9312static LogicalResult
9313convertFreeSharedMemOp(omp::FreeSharedMemOp freeMemOp,
9314 llvm::IRBuilderBase &builder,
9315 LLVM::ModuleTranslation &moduleTranslation) {
9316 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder *ompBuilder = moduleTranslation.getOpenMPBuilder();
9317 llvm::Value *size = getAllocationSize(builder, moduleTranslation, freeMemOp);
9318 ompBuilder->createOMPFreeShared(
9319 builder, moduleTranslation.lookupValue(freeMemOp.getHeapref()), size);
9320 return success();
9321}
9322
9323/// Converts an OpenMP groupprivate operation into LLVM IR.
9324static LogicalResult
9325convertOmpGroupprivate(Operation &opInst, llvm::IRBuilderBase &builder,
9326 LLVM::ModuleTranslation &moduleTranslation) {
9327 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder *ompBuilder = moduleTranslation.getOpenMPBuilder();
9328 auto groupprivateOp = cast<omp::GroupprivateOp>(opInst);
9329
9330 if (failed(checkImplementationStatus(opInst)))
9331 return failure();
9332
9333 bool isTargetDevice = ompBuilder->Config.isTargetDevice();
9334
9335 // Determine whether group-private storage should be allocated based on
9336 // device_type. When not specified, default to 'any' (allocate on both).
9337 bool shouldAllocate = true;
9338 switch (groupprivateOp.getDeviceType().value_or(
9339 mlir::omp::DeclareTargetDeviceType::any)) {
9340 case mlir::omp::DeclareTargetDeviceType::host:
9341 shouldAllocate = !isTargetDevice;
9342 break;
9343 case mlir::omp::DeclareTargetDeviceType::nohost:
9344 shouldAllocate = isTargetDevice;
9345 break;
9346 case mlir::omp::DeclareTargetDeviceType::any:
9347 shouldAllocate = true;
9348 break;
9349 }
9350
9351 // Look up the global variable directly by symbol name.
9352 LLVM::GlobalOp global = SymbolTable::lookupNearestSymbolFrom<LLVM::GlobalOp>(
9353 &opInst, groupprivateOp.getSymNameAttr());
9354 if (!global)
9355 return opInst.emitError()
9356 << "expected symbol '" << groupprivateOp.getSymName()
9357 << "' to reference an LLVM global variable";
9358
9359 llvm::GlobalValue *globalValue = moduleTranslation.lookupGlobal(global);
9360 llvm::Type *varType = moduleTranslation.convertType(global.getType());
9361 std::string varName = globalValue->getName().str();
9362
9363 llvm::Value *resultPtr;
9364 if (shouldAllocate && isTargetDevice) {
9365 llvm::Module *llvmModule = moduleTranslation.getLLVMModule();
9366 llvm::Triple targetTriple(llvmModule->getTargetTriple());
9367 unsigned sharedAddressSpace;
