9368 if (targetTriple.isAMDGCN())
9369 sharedAddressSpace = llvm::AMDGPUAS::LOCAL_ADDRESS;
9370 else if (targetTriple.isNVPTX())
9371 sharedAddressSpace = llvm::NVPTXAS::ADDRESS_SPACE_SHARED;
9372 else
9373 return opInst.emitError() << "groupprivate is not supported for target: "
9374 << targetTriple.str();
9375 llvm::GlobalVariable *sharedVar = new llvm::GlobalVariable(
9376 *llvmModule, varType, /*isConstant=*/false,
9377 llvm::GlobalValue::InternalLinkage, llvm::PoisonValue::get(varType),
9378 varName, /*InsertBefore=*/nullptr, llvm::GlobalValue::NotThreadLocal,
9379 sharedAddressSpace,
9380 /*isExternallyInitialized=*/false);
9381 resultPtr = sharedVar;
9382 } else {
9383 if (shouldAllocate && !isTargetDevice)
9384 opInst.emitWarning("groupprivate directive is currently ignored on the "
9385 "host, using original global");
9386 resultPtr = globalValue;
9387 }
9388
9389 moduleTranslation.mapValue(opInst.getResult(0), resultPtr);
9390 return success();
9391}
9392
9393/// Given an OpenMP MLIR operation, create the corresponding LLVM IR (including
9394/// OpenMP runtime calls).
9395LogicalResult OpenMPDialectLLVMIRTranslationInterface::convertOperation(
9396 Operation *op, llvm::IRBuilderBase &builder,
9397 LLVM::ModuleTranslation &moduleTranslation) const {
9398 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder *ompBuilder = moduleTranslation.getOpenMPBuilder();
9399
9400 if (ompBuilder->Config.isTargetDevice() &&
9401 !isa<omp::TargetOp, omp::MapInfoOp, omp::TerminatorOp, omp::YieldOp>(
9402 op) &&
9403 isHostDeviceOp(op))
9404 return op->emitOpError() << "unsupported host op found in device";
9405
9406 // For each loop, introduce one stack frame to hold loop information. Ensure
9407 // this is only done for the outermost loop wrapper to prevent introducing
9408 // multiple stack frames for a single loop. Initially set to null, the loop
9409 // information structure is initialized during translation of the nested
9410 // omp.loop_nest operation, making it available to translation of all loop
9411 // wrappers after their body has been successfully translated.
9412 bool isOutermostLoopWrapper =
9413 isa_and_present<omp::LoopWrapperInterface>(op) &&
9414 !dyn_cast_if_present<omp::LoopWrapperInterface>(op->getParentOp());
9415
9416 // The TASKLOOP construct is implemented with an outer taskloop.context
9417 // operation which is not a loop wrapper, containing an inner taskloop
9418 // operation which is a loop wrapper. The stack frame should be pushed when
9419 // translating the outer taskloop.context and popped when translating the
9420 // inner taskloop which is a loop wrapper. We need access to the loop
9421 // information in the outer taskloop context so we need to create it and pop
9422 // it around the taskloop context not the inner loop wrapper.
9423 if (isa<omp::TaskloopContextOp>(op))
9424 isOutermostLoopWrapper = true;
9425 else if (isa<omp::TaskloopWrapperOp>(op))
9426 isOutermostLoopWrapper = false;
9427
9428 if (isOutermostLoopWrapper)
9429 moduleTranslation.stackPush<OpenMPLoopInfoStackFrame>();
9430
9431 auto result =
9432 llvm::TypeSwitch<Operation *, LogicalResult>(op)
9433 .Case([&](omp::BarrierOp op) -> LogicalResult {
9434 if (failed(checkImplementationStatus(*op)))
9435 return failure();
9436
9437 llvm::OpenMPIRBuilder::InsertPointOrErrorTy afterIP =
9438 ompBuilder->createBarrier(builder.saveIP(),
9439 llvm::omp::OMPD_barrier);
9440 LogicalResult res = handleError(afterIP, *op);
9441 if (res.succeeded()) {
9442 // If the barrier generated a cancellation check, the insertion
9443 // point might now need to be changed to a new continuation block
9444 builder.restoreIP(*afterIP);
9445 }
9446 return res;
9447 })
9448 .Case([&](omp::TaskyieldOp op) {
9449 if (failed(checkImplementationStatus(*op)))
9450 return failure();
9451
9452 ompBuilder->createTaskyield(builder.saveIP());
9453 return success();
9454 })
9455 .Case([&](omp::FlushOp op) {
9456 if (failed(checkImplementationStatus(*op)))
9457 return failure();
9458
9459 // No support in Openmp runtime function (__kmpc_flush) to accept
9460 // the argument list.
9461 // OpenMP standard states the following:
9462 // "An implementation may implement a flush with a list by ignoring
9463 // the list, and treating it the same as a flush without a list."
9464 //
9465 // The argument list is discarded so that, flush with a list is
9466 // treated same as a flush without a list.
9467 ompBuilder->createFlush(builder.saveIP());
9468 return success();
9469 })
9470 .Case([&](omp::ParallelOp op) {
9471 return convertOmpParallel(op, builder, moduleTranslation);
9472 })
9473 .Case([&](omp::MaskedOp) {
9474 return convertOmpMasked(*op, builder, moduleTranslation);
9475 })
9476 .Case([&](omp::MasterOp) {
9477 return convertOmpMaster(*op, builder, moduleTranslation);
9478 })
9479 .Case([&](omp::CriticalOp) {
9480 return convertOmpCritical(*op, builder, moduleTranslation);
9481 })
9482 .Case([&](omp::OrderedRegionOp) {
9483 return convertOmpOrderedRegion(*op, builder, moduleTranslation);
9484 })
9485 .Case([&](omp::OrderedOp) {
9486 return convertOmpOrdered(*op, builder, moduleTranslation);
9487 })
9488 .Case([&](omp::WsloopOp) {
9489 return convertOmpWsloop(*op, builder, moduleTranslation);
9490 })
9491 .Case([&](omp::SimdOp) {
9492 return convertOmpSimd(*op, builder, moduleTranslation);
9493 })
9494 .Case([&](omp::AtomicReadOp) {
9495 return convertOmpAtomicRead(*op, builder, moduleTranslation);
9496 })
9497 .Case([&](omp::AtomicWriteOp) {
9498 return convertOmpAtomicWrite(*op, builder, moduleTranslation);
9499 })
9500 .Case([&](omp::AtomicUpdateOp op) {
9501 return convertOmpAtomicUpdate(op, builder, moduleTranslation);
9502 })
9503 .Case([&](omp::AtomicCaptureOp op) {
9504 return convertOmpAtomicCapture(op, builder, moduleTranslation);
9505 })
9506 .Case([&](omp::AtomicCompareOp op) {
9507 return convertOmpAtomicCompare(op, builder, moduleTranslation);
9508 })
9509 .Case([&](omp::CancelOp op) {
9510 return convertOmpCancel(op, builder, moduleTranslation);
9511 })
9512 .Case([&](omp::CancellationPointOp op) {
9513 return convertOmpCancellationPoint(op, builder, moduleTranslation);
9514 })
9515 .Case([&](omp::SectionsOp) {
9516 return convertOmpSections(*op, builder, moduleTranslation);
9517 })
9518 .Case([&](omp::ScopeOp op) {
9519 return convertOmpScope(op, builder, moduleTranslation);
9520 })
9521 .Case([&](omp::SingleOp op) {
9522 return convertOmpSingle(op, builder, moduleTranslation);
9523 })
9524 .Case([&](omp::TeamsOp op) {
9525 return convertOmpTeams(op, builder, moduleTranslation);
9526 })
9527 .Case([&](omp::TaskOp op) {
9528 return convertOmpTaskOp(op, builder, moduleTranslation);
9529 })
9530 .Case([&](omp::TaskloopWrapperOp op) {
9531 return convertOmpTaskloopWrapperOp(op, builder, moduleTranslation);
9532 })
9533 .Case([&](omp::TaskloopContextOp op) {
9534 return convertOmpTaskloopContextOp(op, builder, moduleTranslation);
9535 })
9536 .Case([&](omp::TaskgroupOp op) {
9537 return convertOmpTaskgroupOp(op, builder, moduleTranslation);
9538 })
9539 .Case([&](omp::TaskwaitOp op) {
9540 return convertOmpTaskwaitOp(op, builder, moduleTranslation);
9541 })
9542 .Case<omp::YieldOp, omp::TerminatorOp, omp::DeclareMapperOp,
9543 omp::DeclareMapperInfoOp, omp::DeclareReductionOp,
9544 omp::CriticalDeclareOp>([](auto op) {
9545 // `yield` and `terminator` can be just omitted. The block structure
9546 // was created in the region that handles their parent operation.
9547 // `declare_reduction` will be used by reductions and is not
9548 // converted directly, skip it.
9549 // `declare_mapper` and `declare_mapper.info` are handled whenever
9550 // they are referred to through a `map` clause.
9551 // `critical.declare` is only used to declare names of critical
9552 // sections which will be used by `critical` ops and hence can be
9553 // ignored for lowering. The OpenMP IRBuilder will create unique
9554 // name for critical section names.
9555 return success();
9556 })
9557 .Case([&](omp::ThreadprivateOp) {
9558 return convertOmpThreadprivate(*op, builder, moduleTranslation);
9559 })
9560 .Case<omp::TargetDataOp, omp::TargetEnterDataOp,
9561 omp::TargetExitDataOp, omp::TargetUpdateOp>([&](auto op) {
9562 return convertOmpTargetData(op, builder, moduleTranslation);
9563 })
9564 .Case([&](omp::TargetOp) {
9565 return convertOmpTarget(*op, builder, moduleTranslation);
9566 })
9567 .Case([&](omp::DistributeOp) {
9568 return convertOmpDistribute(*op, builder, moduleTranslation);
9569 })
9570 .Case([&](omp::LoopNestOp) {
9571 return convertOmpLoopNest(*op, builder, moduleTranslation);
9572 })
9573 .Case<omp::MapInfoOp, omp::MapBoundsOp, omp::PrivateClauseOp,
9574 omp::AffinityEntryOp, omp::IteratorOp>([&](auto op) {
9575 // No-op, should be handled by relevant owning operations e.g.
9576 // TargetOp, TargetEnterDataOp, TargetExitDataOp, TargetDataOp
9577 // etc. and then discarded
9578 return success();
9579 })
9580 .Case([&](omp::NewCliOp op) {
9581 // Meta-operation: Doesn't do anything by itself, but used to
9582 // identify a loop.
9583 return success();
9584 })
9585 .Case([&](omp::CanonicalLoopOp op) {
9586 return convertOmpCanonicalLoopOp(op, builder, moduleTranslation);
9587 })
9588 .Case([&](omp::UnrollHeuristicOp op) {
9589 // FIXME: Handling omp.unroll_heuristic as an executable requires
9590 // that the generator (e.g. omp.canonical_loop) has been seen first.
9591 // For construct that require all codegen to occur inside a callback
9592 // (e.g. OpenMPIRBilder::createParallel), all codegen of that
9593 // contained region including their transformations must occur at
9594 // the omp.canonical_loop.
9595 return applyUnrollHeuristic(op, builder, moduleTranslation);
9596 })
9597 .Case([&](omp::TileOp op) {
9598 return applyTile(op, builder, moduleTranslation);
9599 })
9600 .Case([&](omp::FuseOp op) {
9601 return applyFuse(op, builder, moduleTranslation);
9602 })
9603 .Case([&](omp::TargetAllocMemOp) {
9604 return convertTargetAllocMemOp(*op, builder, moduleTranslation);
9605 })
9606 .Case([&](omp::TargetFreeMemOp) {
9607 return convertTargetFreeMemOp(*op, builder, moduleTranslation);
9608 })
9609 .Case([&](omp::AllocateDirOp) {
9610 return convertAllocateDirOp(*op, builder, moduleTranslation, *this);
9611 })
9612 .Case([&](omp::AllocateFreeOp) {
9613 return convertAllocateFreeOp(*op, builder, moduleTranslation,
9614 *this);
9615 })
9616 .Case([&](omp::AllocSharedMemOp op) {
9617 return convertAllocSharedMemOp(op, builder, moduleTranslation);
9618 })
9619 .Case([&](omp::FreeSharedMemOp op) {
9620 return convertFreeSharedMemOp(op, builder, moduleTranslation);
9621 })
9622 .Case([&](omp::GroupprivateOp) {
9623 return convertOmpGroupprivate(*op, builder, moduleTranslation);
9624 })
9625 .Default([&](Operation *inst) {
9626 return inst->emitError()
9627 << "not yet implemented: " << inst->getName();
9628 });
9629
9630 if (isOutermostLoopWrapper)
9631 moduleTranslation.stackPop();
9632
9633 return result;
9634}
9635
9636void mlir::registerOpenMPDialectTranslation(DialectRegistry &registry) {
9637 registry.insert<omp::OpenMPDialect>();
9638 registry.addExtension(+[](MLIRContext *ctx, omp::OpenMPDialect *dialect) {
9639 dialect->addInterfaces<OpenMPDialectLLVMIRTranslationInterface>();
9640 });
9641}
9642
9643void mlir::registerOpenMPDialectTranslation(MLIRContext &context) {
9644 DialectRegistry registry;
9645 registerOpenMPDialectTranslation(registry);
9646 context.appendDialectRegistry(registry);
9647}
